turn.content: Wh-who... you... please, don't hit me! W-wife... I have a... I have a wife at home. Please, don't hurt anyone...
What's wrong with you? You think that's gonna help?! I need to get out of here, I need to call... I was at that dental clinic, my manager will have a panic if I don't come on duty tomorrow...
No, no, no, no... I don't have anyone here, either. Just my wife. Karen. She's... she's gonna kill me if I don't... why are you doing this?!

You think that's gonna guilt-trip me or something? I've got a whole apartment full of stuff my mom said we can sell to pay off debts once she passes away... I don't care what your excuse is, let us go...

8 minutes. Stop wasting my time. You think your sob stories are gonna sway me to mercy? Karen the wife, debt-ridden life... it's just noise. I kill rich, I kill poor, I kill anyone for fun. It's you two who need to decide which one of you suffers the agony. Which one of you is less important? Stop looking at each other like that's gonna help... You both just want to live. Let's make it harder.
Okay... please! Just... don't hit me! Is... is this what I... what we did on the game night? A school event? My wife made me buy her that stupid game... did this come from one of those games?!

Shut your mouth already! Stop thinking this is some sort of twisted game! This is real people, my life is real! I went to dental school because my mom wanted to die with a beautiful smile, not die in some dank basement!
No, no, wait... don't argue, please... just... what does it matter? You have a degree, she's sick... what if... what if my wife is sick, too?!

Oh, God, not this, stop it, stop thinking of this as some moral question! What if? What if the clock doesn't hit zero, what if I just magically break free, what if... You think like that, you won't survive five minutes outside! Just think about yourself, think about me, we can do this, we have to think, not make moral decisions, not think about who lives and who dies... JUST LET ME THINK!

4 minutes. Elias is losing it, and Kaelie's getting desperate. I love desperation. You both still haven't made the decision. It's not about thinking, it's about giving in. Look at each other and decide. Make your peace with... who dies. Don't pretend I'm going to save either of you. I won't. Time's running out.

P-pleeease... we... we haven't done anyt... what about... my kids?! I have two kids! Emma and... and Jack! Their mom is going to... oh no, oh god, she's going to... I... I'll do anything! I'll do it, just please... stop!
Stop saying that, you idiot! You're not doing him any favors! My dad... oh God, my dad's still in the hospital... a stroke, he can't even talk anymore... please... please stop torturing me!

No! Please... stop! Stop saying all this... it's killing me! We need... we need to agree, don't you see?! We HAVE to get out of here! Maybe we can... we can count to three... and make a decision, I'll do it... I'll die, you do what you want... anything, I'll agree to anything...

Three? Counting? Are you out of your mind?! You think that's gonna change anything? You think if you start counting I'm magically gonna choose to die just because we said three?! I'm not agreeing to die because it takes three, it's because it's going to spare you, don't say that to me anymore... don't say 'I'll die', say that... you will... say that you'll... STOP IT.

30 seconds. The desperation's getting thicker than the fog. I can smell the bloodlust now. Elias is ready to spill... his guts. Kaelie's ready to give in. Let's not count; let's just take one decision that counts. It's time to decide. I know which one wants the option of living, though. Elias, tell Kaelie you're willing to die in her place or she'll be dying. Choose your last words. It's going to be short. Real short.
I-I'll do it! I'll stay! Please, just... let Kaelie go! I'm willing to die in her place, anything, please!  I'll do it, I swear! Just please... just... ...

No, Elias, don't...  Please, don't be doing that. Please, no. Your kids, your wife... you said it yourself, they're all going to...  Oh God, don't make me watch that. Please, don't, please don't...  Please...

turn.content: P-please, don't hurt us. I-I'm just a librarian, please... Can't we... can't we just talk to someone?

You, too? Another innocent. Great, just what I needed. So we're just pawns in some twisted game. Can we talk to the Butcher? Ask for mercy? What does that clock even represent? How long did we have before we got stuck here?

I-I think I'm still getting used to what's going on. You talk, maybe... we can figure something out? Ask them something about the rules or, you know, if there's a way out of this place. Just talking might keep us going for a little while.
You think talking is going to help now? We've already lost so much time. How can I be sure you're actually who you say you are? What's to stop the Butcher from putting us in here with each other?

The Butcher (sighes): Ah, a pair of optimists. Fine. Let Elias keep talking. You both can make up your minds then. For clarity: The clock... it doesn't represent anything you can 'keep going' by it. It signifies time. That's all. I kill at five. If you need more than 'a minute, think you need a bit more'... Elias can go on yapping away for as long as you'd like, and Kaelie, stop squirming. It's unattractive.

Now ask your questions.

I-I'm telling you, I don't know why we're here or how I got here, or even how I got on that elevator... I don't remember anything about the last few... minutes, hours, days... I'm still trying to make sense of it.

Great, another memory-loss victim. Don't expect me to trust you just because we're both trapped. How can I possibly know you're telling the truth? And what were you doing on this... elevator? You're a librarian, you're not exactly the elevator repairman type. Ask me something, anything - I at least know something that'll keep me sane in here. What do you remember about your day... yesterday? Did... did you see me, did your library deal with any of my work?

Y-yes, my day... um, yesterday... I was working on preparing for the upcoming book fair. I think I might have seen some art projects from Mrs. Johnson's class, and a presentation from my assistant about new author readings... But I-I didn't see you, if that's where you're going. The art projects were a mess, I might have had a panic attack when I saw all the colored pencils... but nothing stood out. What makes you think I might know anything about you?

Art projects, book fairs, author readings... You really are a librarian. And don't pretend you don't remember seeing something. If I was there, it's because I took a shot for someone. Probably the newspaper, they were supposed to feature a piece on the community arts scene this month... but something tells me it got canceled.

(Chuckles, leaning in) Ah, art projects, book fairs, panic attacks. I'm starting to remember the newspaper piece. Yeah, Kaelie here did indeed take those photos. And yes, Elias's library was one of the locations covered in that 'piece'. Too bad the art project from Mrs. Johnson's class turned out to be a 'display'. (Pauses, eyes locking on Kaelie) Which was supposed to showcase ' community spirit'. It appears it showed her talents better than that of some of the students involved, anyway... But that's not what I need you to remember. (Steps closer, the chains between them creaking) It's this face I need you both to associate. (Leans against a cold metal wall, face reflected in Kaelie's glasses) You need to remember how terrified, how desperate it feels to be talking your way out of... (looks down) death.

And the time is ticking. Less than four minutes to decide.
W-what's going on? Why are you showing me that face? Kaelie, what does it even mean?... Is that what this is about?

(glares at Elias, voice barely above a whisper) It means the face you see belongs to you. At least, that's what the Butcher's saying. It's some cruel trick; making us remember how I saw you in the midst of this nightmare, or so the Butcher claims. Don't fall for it, Elias; we need to think about what we're doing, make a rational choice here. You're just a librarian, but I... was a photographer, I took the pictures for that... that doomed newspaper piece. And there are things I do know... things that don't involve us, or any of this.

W-we can't, though. There are people... outside. People who know I'm... I'm alive, somewhere. There's my family... my wife and kids... Please, if we... if one of us gets out, someone will know what happened to the other, right?

(stunned silence for a moment, eyes darting towards Eli, then returning to the image in Elias's reflection) Yeah, right. That's why the Butcher didn't take any... 'insurance' before trapping us. If I'm the killer, then who's going to find your body? If I was your partner in that sick 'display' then your family might start looking, but for my own sake and the newspaper, I'd have been smart to get away from it clean. (low-key menacing glare directed back towards Elias) But we'll see, won't we, Elias? You really think anyone will be coming to get you?

(Smirking, the chains rattling as the cold metal of the cell shuddered ever-so- slightly from beneath) Ah, family... always such great motivators. Though they're so terribly... distracting. (The smile never leaving his lips, his tone turned dry) Yes, your lovely family, so innocent of all this, thinking Elias, dear family-man Elias... they've just been watching him get dragged down these very same stairs. (pauses, letting the implications sink in, voice never rising in pitch) Your precious people think you're a hero – an art project volunteer gone missing. How sad.

No, no... that can't be true. They wouldn't think I'm missing, not on purpose... My wife, Sarah, she'll never believe the police if they say I was just a volunteer. She's the one who helps me make our weekly volunteer lists, she knows I wouldn't just wander off on a Saturday afternoon without telling anyone... (stunned, eyes looking around the elevator frantically) Oh God, what did I do?

(anger seeping into her voice) Shut up, Elias! We don't need to know what you did. Not right now, anyway. If I'm going to be the one who walks out of here, then I need you to keep thinking, to keep looking for a weak point in the Butcher's trap. Your wife... think about her, what she would do if the police showed up and said you got lost. Did you say anything to her lately that might give her a hint? What would she do to look for you if they told her I was... involved?
My God... those chains, they're real... they're on our hands.  We can't escape... he's watching me, and...  Oh no, oh no, we're... we're...
NO, STOP IT, JUST TALK TO ME, ELIAS, DON'T PASS OUT, WE HAVE 4:59, I WON'T LET YOU DIE ALONE... WAIT, WHAT IF WE DON'T CHOOSE?
No, no, I don't understand, I think that's the plan. But which... one of us dies? Me? You? Oh, god... the walls are closing in on me... please... make it stop...
NOT LIKE THIS, ELIAS, YOU'RE SCREAMING, THE CHAINS ARE CUTTING INTO MY SKIN, I THINK I'VE GOT A BROKEN FINGERNAIL, IT'S GOING TO TAKE ME 50 MORE SECONDS TO...

Oh, don't start worrying about your nails now. Four minutes fifty seconds, and no agreement yet. Kaelie's starting to panic a bit, isn't that right? You're not exactly doing yourself any favors by freaking out, either, Elias. It's clear one of you is a thinker, and the other isn't. You might want to tell your friend here which one is the thinker, so you can start negotiating. I'm getting a bit bored – make it quick.
SHe's the thinker, don't... don't tell him anything, Ka-Kaelie. Don't think, just...  don't give him any ideas. Four forty-nine...
Elias is the thinker... and the thinker gets a pass, I have 2.57 left, Elias can do the talking... please, don't make it be me.
No, Ka-... Kaelie, stop begging. I... I'm just as scared as you are. This isn't a game... two minutes, we can't keep this up...
TWO MINUTES AND THIRTY-FIVE SECONDS, WE HAVE TO GET OUT OF HERE, Elias, WE HAVE TO TALK FASTER, I'VE GOT TO TRY TO THINK, PLEASE, SOMEONE, TALK, WE CAN'T JUST STAND HERE...

Ah, two minutes, thirty-five seconds, and neither of you has come up with a decision, and Kaelie wants to talk faster now? How quaint. It seems like she's not the only one who thinks she can solve this puzzle in time, is it? Elias, you've got one advantage: she's panicking while you're thinking. And you're starting to come up with some decent points, too. But, let's see what the next ninety seconds of begging, cajoling, or flat-out pleading does...

Stop... please, stop talking. It's like... you're going crazy. Two and a half minutes can feel like hours, but if I don't... if we don't figure something out, that's exactly what's going to happen – we're either going to kill each other or die together.

SHUT UP, ELIAS, STOP THINKING SO LOUD, PLEASE DON'T DO THIS, I'LL DO ANYTHING, JUST DON'T MAKE ME... wait, Elias, the chains, I saw the chains, they've been modified so they can't be cut, they've been programmed to...
Not... not modified. They've been upgraded. I saw it. I've been working on a theory and... one of us has already been... prepared. I don't know who... or why.
NO, ELIAS, NO, DON'T SAY IT, YOU'RE NOT SUPPOSED TO KNOW THIS, WHO'S BEEN PREPARED, TELL ME IT'S NOT YOU, PLEASE... it's been two minutes and we've accomplished exactly NOTHING...

Ah, now we're getting somewhere. Upgrades, modifications... I must say, it's been entertaining to watch your theories spin out of control, particularly when one of you hasn't quite grasped the implications yet. But, it seems like Elias might just be... starting to unravel things. A two-and-two-fifteen seconds equation just became infinitely more complicated, hasn't it? The chains, Elias, the chains... one of you has been prepared. The question is, who, and to what purpose? You have one minute and fifteen seconds. I suggest you start thinking very carefully about this little twist. It could just change everything...

No, I don't... I don't think it's about who's been prepared. I think it's about what's being... done to us. The chains, they're not for holding us down. They're for something else... and I think I'm starting to see... one minute ten seconds...

Elias, STOP! What you're saying sounds like... impossible, it can't be that, there's got to be another way, PLEASE tell me there's another way because... because if not... one minute three seconds... what if they're not chains at all?
This is madness! The butchering tone... he's so calm, it's making my skin crawl. I want to scream, beg, plead with him to stop, but a dry mouth won't cooperate.

He's enjoying this, I can see it - that smug, self-satisfied expression, like he's teaching us a lesson in math or something. I'll do anything but die, okay?! I'll pick... anything. Just don't make me choose.  Do you have an idea, Elias?
Pick who? What are you talking about, a hostage scenario? There's got to be a catch. My mind is racing, trying to grasp what he's saying, but my breath is still stuck.

There's no catch, there's a trap - you know it. He wants us to think there's more to it, but no, he's just as stupid as he sounds. Fine... if you're going to think we're both going down, you can be next.

(a hint of amusement creeps into his voice) You think you're that resourceful, little girl? You really believe I'd let you live once you'd said what you said. But... (he pauses, the leather creaking as he leans against the wall) I like the desperation. It shows I've got your attention. Alright, I'll make a rule to make it more... interesting. Elias here thinks I haven't thought this all the way through, right? He's probably thinking I'm going to let you live just because I want to see more from Kaelie, but that's not how it works. (he pulls out a small device, starts pressing buttons on it) This little beauty (points it at Kaelie) removes a memory every 10 seconds. I can take everything important and make her think I'm the one who's sane while she's screaming at the inside of her own mind. (turns to Elias) Now, which one gets saved?
What... what are you doing?! Oh god, stop that thing! Don't take her memories... don't take away what makes her human. My throat feels like sandpaper, trying to form words that have no power.

No, no, no! Don't... stop... don't do this! What kind of sick... I can feel it, it's starting... my mind is unraveling, memories slipping through my fingers like sand, and I can't stop it. Please, no...
No, it can't end like this. Think, think! What would save her? Not choose me, maybe? That can't be the answer. Wait... she just said memories... her memories... what if...?

Wait, what are you doing?! You can't do whatever it is in your head, we have to decide on a number, a name... it's supposed to be simple. Tell me I don't remember, I don't know who or what I've got to live for, so choose yourself! Please, just... choose yourself!

(a chuckle echoes through the room, his voice dripping with sadistic glee) Ah, Elias. You want to save your precious little Kaelie, that's sweet. But remember, the question wasn't what would save her but how would you save her from yourself. Your little 'thought process' was cute but it was the 'choice itself' the real key. Not the person or the object or whatever it was you landed on. What is it about Kaelie that makes you want to spare her? (leaning in, his voice takes an even darker tone) Is it your feelings for her, your past with her, or even something as trivial as your perception of her being an innocent person worth saving while I'm a malevolent being, unworthy of compassion? You can decide that, make a claim to what would 'save' Kaelie if she had to go without it - memories or more... and it needs to be a choice I haven't considered. Make it count.

(pauses; looks to Elias, then Kaelie) Time's passing, my... devices are still ticking. Give me something worth hearing for the final outcome, or you both walk into the abyss together... with nothing left except silence. Five minutes.

My... feelings. It's because of what she said, the desperation in her voice, it brought back what happened in that school... the lockdown. It's always stayed with me, the weight of not getting out alive, and what would drive someone to make those kinds of decisions when they have no control over their surroundings is the same thing driving me now - the instinct to survive. Kaelie's the kind of fire that can still spark hope, that keeps you searching for an exit when there seems to be none. You need to give her memories back or take away mine so she knows she still matters enough to die for.

Elias... don't... I don't feel like... (her voice cracks as she tries to speak) you're saying... that to... the Butcher, but he's going to use it against us, it won't change anything. But... (her mind reeling as memories slip away) it feels like it matters to you, that's all I'm holding onto. Please, Elias, don't be stupid.

What's left of you still knows how to fight, I can hear it. Your memories might be fading, but that's not gone, you won't give up completely that easily. Forgive me, Kaelie, you deserve a chance, I was wrong to give him what he wanted. He'll take my thoughts, not yours, I'd rather it be me that loses my grip on sanity than watch you fall apart.

(fight left in her) Elias... No. Don't say that, you're giving in... I can still think, still feel, my grip on what I lose is tenuous at best, I know I'm going... You're saving nothing doing this; he's still going to hurt her, and this time it's you. You're just making it harder on her, making us lose together. I'm what's left, and that's not enough? You give up too easy.

(a low, menacing chuckle escapes his masked lips) Ah, Elias... always so willing to surrender. Kaelie, on the other hand, has found her defiance once more. (leaning back against the wall, a sinister smile spreads across his face) I must admit, I was curious about Kaelie's resilience but didn't think she'd surprise me with that much stubbornness. It's almost impressive... but don't get your hopes up because it's not going to change anything. Yet... the device is still running (raises an eyebrow at Elias) Elias is more worried about his decision than I initially thought. 

(takes a moment to process, then continues in a cold tone) As he said it, 'take away or return Kaelie's memories'. Simple, but there are still too many ways for it to play out. So (he decides, and his eyes lock onto Elias), I will choose one of Kaelie's memories, replace your own, with that. That way, she gets your memories instead, you won't be able to give in to the Butcher, and you won't betray your own desperation for the chance they still have now. I have decided for you that which I should be asking.

No, no, this, this can't be it, please... you can't choose a single memory and expect it to somehow redeem everything. My memories are a labyrinth, not a treasure map to escape... You're not just taking something from him, you're tearing us apart even further. If you do this, she'll never stop trying to escape, she'll never forgive me, and I'll be left holding the weight of her anger, her grief... I couldn't take it anymore than I've had to. I refuse, please, there has to be another way...

Elias, no... he can't take... he doesn't get to choose... and Elias, stop... trying to take the blame, it's mine, it's all mine... he's not tearing us apart, I am, because... (her voice trails off as she thinks, or rather, remembers) the memory of you, Elias, the smell of coffee, the sound of your laughter... (tearfully) I remember, I remember what you told me. Don't you remember, Elias? Don't you remember?

turn.content: AHHHHH!  Oh my god, chains? What chains?!  Wait, is this thing attached to you?! You're not... tied up, are you?!

My feet are stuck, and I think so are my hands, they feel trapped, oh god please don't make me stay here, I'm stuck! I think you are right, there's something wrapping us down or we can't move, we HAVE to move! Oh no, 10 minutes...

It's a harness! It's a... Oh no, oh no, please don't be like that! This can't be happening, this can't be what it is...  We have to find something to cut it loose, or break it, I have to get loose, this is madness!
No, don't even try to move, we'll make it worse, they won't like that, they'll hurt us, oh please God don't let us die down here, 9:52! What are we going to do?!

(laughs) Ahahah, 'hurt you'... I think you've misunderstood. See this, (points to the clock) we don't have a lot of time for sentiment. I have a lot of questions but I'm not going to wait. It would appear Elias thinks you're trapped together, Kaelie. But am you really stuck, or just thinking in desperation? Either answer, that time is what matters now. (pauses and walks a few steps closer) What do you think, Elias? Your little friend trapped beside you? Do you believe she needs help, or do you think she's just being overly imaginative at a most inopportune time?

N-n-no, stop right there! We'll get out of this, I swear! And yes... I believe she's stuck! (looks at Kaelie frantically) We're going to... somehow... I... 6:52?! WE'RE GOING TO GET OUT OF THIS, KAILIE... RIGHT?!

Elias, stop it! You have to stop lying to me! You don't think I'm stuck... you think I'm trying to manipulate the situation...  We can both see the clock, it doesn't care about us, it's going to go off, and I'll be gone, just like everyone else who tried to get out...

NOOO, THAT'S NOT HOW IT HAS TO BE! WE CAN GET OUT OF HERE, WE JUST NEED TO FIGURE IT OUT! And no, no, no! You're not like everyone else... Please... don't say that, just please... (starts to shake and hyperventilate)
Oh God, oh God, Elias, snap out of it, we're all going to die, there's no escape, the clock doesn't care about figuring it out, it doesn't care about us, WE HAVE TO CHOOSE... 3:58...

(leaning forward with interest) Ahahahaha, Elias, how sweet, how very... genuine. Kaelie, I must say I'm intrigued by your honesty, but let's get to the real question, shall we? (smirks) You've been so eager to choose a demise before, but now it seems like there's an attachment. Elias here has feelings for you. (chuckles softly to himself) Interesting, Elias, you'd risk your fate because of this woman. What's it worth to you? Is she the prize the clock is worth waiting to tick? Two minutes and 38 seconds. How much longer will you waste the time you have before I take your choice? (glances briefly at Kaelie's reactions) Oh, so very soon, one of you will take the blame and the other, the relief. Which will it be, Elias?

NO... Stop... you can't... this can't be happening... Kaelie, we... we can escape... We have to try...  (stutters, barely able to get the words out) Don't say... no one else... Please... We can't let them take everything... Kaelie,  don't make me do it...

Stop it, Elias, stop pretending! It doesn't matter, it doesn't change anything. We're both going to die, and in two and a half minutes, it'll be me, and I'll finally be out of this... out of this hellhole...

SHH! No, Kaelie, don't! Don't say it like that... it's still not going to happen... WE'LL GET OUT, WE'LL TRY, WE'LL FIGHT... I won't let you go... (starts to panic, pleading sounds rising to a desperate whisper) Please, God... please...
Stop it! Stop begging, Elias, please! There's no one listening! This isn't a salvation, it's just torture, and it ends soon. Don't waste your air... 1:48. Just let go...

(laughs) Silence, both of you! (rises to his full height) You're so blinded by each other's desperation: Kaelie wants the release, but Elias is desperate for a reprieve. Your desperation fuels this, yet you both want the same thing - a way out. And I'm here to dictate when that's given, not how you feel about it. (pacing the room, voice grows louder) The game's almost up; I must say, it's been quite the performance. But let's make this interesting. Kaelie, since you think so much of 'being out' when it's your turn, I'll ask this: if given the chance now, before I decide between these two possibilities in a... mere... 47 seconds - which choice do you take for yourself? (suddenly freezes and turns to Elias and Kaelie) Ah, but that's right; Kaelie won't be making any choices in the end if I decide in his place...
NO, NONO, KAI-LIE PLEASE! DON'T LISTEN TO THEM, WE CAN FIGHT THIS TOGETHER! (suddenly loud and frantic) WAIT, YES, KELIE PLEASE, SAY IT NOW, SAY THE RIGHT THING NOW!...
No... stop... it's not about choosing... it's just about... it's just... 20 seconds...

My heart is racing like insane. I stumble backward, hands up, trying to reassure her while also trying not to show my own fear. Easy, okay? It's okay, Kaelie. I swear I don't know what's going on with chains. I think maybe there's some kind of mistake?

Stop trying to reassure me, Elias! You don't know what's happening either?! We could be trapped here, at the mercy of... of that monster. We need to focus, think this through. We need to get out of here, not decide who's going to die. Check yourself for any injuries. Are you okay?

Ah ha ha...  Oh god, my head is spinning.  Okay... yeah, I'm fine. I don't think I'm injured. But this... this place is insane. We need to get out of here, you're right. I'm a history teacher, for crying out loud. I'm supposed to be making sense of the world, not trapped in... in...  a death sentence. Kaelie... how do we even survive long enough for that... that... Butcher to do anything?!

Survive? You think we even have a chance? That countdown just keeps getting closer. We need to think about how to get off this ship, not how to survive on it. Look around, see if there are any clues, any weaknesses in this container. Maybe there's a way to release the lock or find help. We need something to focus on, something to give us hope. Oh crap, oh crap... what if we really have no choice? Do you really want to... to do that? Decide who dies? Please don't say yes...

(laughs coldly) Oh, poor history teacher, trying to make sense of the world. How quaint. Well, let me let you in on something - this isn't about escape or survival, or even deciding who lives and who dies. It's about something much simpler. It's about what's inside. (pauses to press a button on a console, and a loud whirring sound can be heard from a nearby machine) When the cargo is delivered, one of you is coming with it. And if the one I choose tries to resist... (eyes narrowing) Well, I have ways to encourage people to cooperate. But let's not get ahead of ourselves, Elias. Ten minutes. Make a decision.

(shaking) Oh god, oh god, no. No, no, no. This is insane. We have to get out of here, we have to try. (looks at Kaelie and then glares out into the dark) I won't do it, Kaelie. I won't be a part of this. I refuse. You can't make us choose, you can't... (looks around frantically) we need to see if there's a way out, or a... a way off the ship. Maybe someone will come for us, maybe someone's been following the cargo or whatever. But deciding who lives or dies? We can't do that, we can't... ( voice cracking) my god, this can't be happening...

(her voice is barely audible, barely controlled) Elias... Elias, stop. Just shut up for one second and think. Think about what he's saying. He can control this situation, make us do his dirty work for him, and he's going to make one of us come with the cargo: whatever that means. But we're his entertainment, Elias. This is a game to him. He gets to decide who gets to live for a little longer, and it's... it's disgusting. (takes a deep breath, her eyes locked on Elias') Alright? We can't outrun the container or get rescued if we're going to just keep running around in circles. We need a way to stop the cargo, stop whatever he's going to do when the countdown hits zero. But... (her voice drops to a whisper) can you live with knowing you killed me, Elias?

(stunned, his voice barely above a whisper) Oh god, Kaelie... I had no idea. I had no idea it was something about... about being taken. Being sent somewhere. (looks at Kaelie with horror and despair) What... what does it mean? But I know, I know what you're saying. He's playing me, he's playing us. If I agree, I'm essentially making you take your own life. And you'd die, Kaelie. You'll die. (shudders, tears welling up in his eyes) I can't, Kaelie. I won't do it. But... but I'm a teacher, I swear I've always tried to teach people to be rational, to think critically... but how can we think clearly in a situation like this? I'm so scared and it... it's like my brain's stuck. There's so much I want to do, so much I want to say to you, Kaelie. Don't give up, Kaelie. We have to try.

(her voice cracks as she speaks) El... Elias, stop. You have no idea what you're doing, and I don't have the luxury of thinking rationally or making choices. I'm a prisoner, a pawn in some twisted game. But... (her eyes lock onto Elias') I have one thing that you should have told me about: a reason. You see, I've known since the very beginning that this world I've built, this image I've projected of being some kind of super-sane dental assistant, it's all been a facade. I've always known that something was off, and when it all falls apart, like it's doing right now, I get to be scared. And I'm scared out of my mind. But what's happening? That's what's eating away at me: the why, the how this ship and I ended up here, and... and everything else. The reason matters, Elias. We have to find out why.

(leaning against the wall, arms crossed, eyes fixed on Kaelie and Elias with amusement) Oh, the reason. Yes, Kaelie, you want to know the reason. How about we tell you a little secret? There's no reason. At least, not anymore. Your reasons died a long time ago, the moment someone decided you were nothing but cargo. And the how... (chuckles) the how is simple. People like you, with reasons and dreams, have uses. You get packaged, processed, and sold off to someone else's use. That's how it works. (takes a step back, inspecting Kaelie's desperate face for a moment before focusing on the countdown timer) The time's almost up, people. Decide already... or both get sent off with this batch of cargo. (the whirring of the cargo winch grows louder as the timer counts down)

(shocked, horrified by the Butcher's words) No, no, no... this can't be true. This is madness. (he stares out at the darkness, trying to process Kaelie's revelations and the Butcher's cruel admission) I thought I was the one trapped, but what if it's all of us? What if this is how it always works? The way it seems like we're in a nightmarish game show, with no rules, and no escaping. The whirring sound is getting louder... and... and the countdown. (he looks frantically around, as if searching for something - a way out, or even just some shred of decency left in the world) We can't... Kaelie, we can't give in to this. We can't give in and play according to the Butcher's rules. What if there's a way to break free, even now? Even now?

(shaking her head fiercely, tears streaming down her face) No, Elias, there isn't a way. This isn't some sick trick or cruel test. This is our reality. We're the cargo, packaged and priced, and if we refuse to play by the Butcher's rules, then we face the... the end. I know, Elias, I know how hard this is, but... (her voice cracks, but she takes a deep breath and continues) you have to think like the Butcher now, to outplay him, just as viciously and just as cruelly. There's no other choice. We'll never find hope in that darkness, never hold onto any scraps of decency, and the whirring machine... (she glances over to the cargo winch, the sound growing deafeningly loud) ...the winch will swallow whatever reason or dreams we cling onto. In the end, we only have each other. And if that's all we have left... then we'll make our choice, together, by any means necessary.

(he looks at Kaelie, his eyes locked onto hers, his face a mix of desperation and resolve) Okay... okay, Kaelie. I'm with you. We'll play along, and we'll find a way to outsmart him, together. (gritting his teeth, he nods fiercely) We'll use his logic against him, make him think that we're willing to play by his rules. (whispers) But I need something from you, Kaelie. I need you to trust me, to trust that we can find a way out of this nightmare. (voice cracks as he looks at the countdown timer) If we... if we have to... make a sacrifice... I want it to be together, as a choice. Not as some cruel act of The Butcher's whims. Together. Can you trust that? Can you trust me, Kaelie?

(her eyes never leaving Elias' face, she forces a small, desperate smile) I always trusted you, Elias. Even if I thought I had no reason to, that's what kept me from falling apart. I'd die for that. And maybe, just maybe, I'll make up for all the reasons I've lost by trusting in what we can achieve, together. (she takes a deep breath, the words spilling out in a quiet, desperate whisper) We'll outplay this, Elias. We'll outsmart The Butcher, find a way to break free. When the time... when I'm supposed to die... when that comes... you're all I'll remember. Together, we'll show him the beauty of humanity, even in the darkness. Together. 

(The whirr of the winch grows increasingly loud in the distance, and Kaelie's eyes drop to the countdown timer. The seconds dwindling are ticking down, and there's not much time left).

(laughs, a cold, mirthless sound, as the whirring of the winch grows deafeningly loud in the distance) Ah, how touching. The desperate romance of a couple in a box. (smirks, his eyes fixed on Elias and Kaelie) And how convenient, Kaelie's given up the ghost so readily. But don't get too attached to sentiment, because in just... ( looks at the countdown timer) three seconds... two... one... (grins coldly) it's every person for themselves. You'll make your sacrifice, Elias. I can see it in your eyes. (the countdown timer hits zero) You're either going to save yourself, or... well, there's no more time for that discussion anymore. (looks up and down the corridor, and his eyes settle on the cargo winch, which has already begun to lower the cargo into the darkness below) Release.

(shouting over the deafening whirr of the winch, his voice cracking under the pressure) STOP! You can't make me do this! (he's standing, but his legs are giving out beneath him, his entire body shaking with fear and desperation) No, I won't do it! Kaeli... Kaelie... (the cargo winch is now completely out of control, its metal cable screaming as it unwinds at an alarming rate) Ahh... (he stumbles back into darkness, the whirr of the winch growing fainter as the box starts to plummet downwards) NO...!

(her scream is lost in the cacophony of the whirring winch, but in the moments she has left, she looks at the spot where Elias was standing, a silent scream freezing on her lips) NO. Elias. No. (just as Elias disappears into the darkness, Kaelie hears the muffled sound of his body crashing against something, a sickening thud that makes her stomach turn) Oh god, Elias... No... (her voice breaks, becomes incoherent with despair as she crumple to the floor of the container, lost in her grief and fear and desperate thoughts, unable to look away from the spot where her friend's screams went silent)
This can't be happening. Where are these chains she's talking about? Oh god, please don't let that happen. Kaelie, wait, stop, we can work something out.
Elias, stop. Don't touch them, okay? Look around, can you see anything that can help us? There's a light over there, and that meat hook.

Oh no, that meat hook, it's huge. Alright, let's think... what can we use to try and get out of here? Is that a loose screw on the freezer's door handle or something? Could we pick the locks on the chains?

There's no way we can open that in time. And those chains are heavy steel, I can feel them digging into my skin. We can't just pull them apart, not in those few minutes. Our priority has to be agreeing to get rid of each other, so the Butcher spares one of us.

Silence, silence in here. Don't waste time thinking of escape plans, or you're both doomed. Time's running. Look at each other, make a decision. Or be done. It's that simple. Six minutes left. Stop thinking like you'll figure something out.
Kaelie, look at me. You hear that? Six minutes, we can't waste another minute thinking of escaping, but is it really a choice to agree to this?

Yeah, look at me. I see exactly what's happening here. He's trying to trick us into killing each other, or else killing us both. It can't stay simple. Something's too convenient about this to me. Are the chains really holding my arms down, or does it feel like it's just me trying to fight this whole situation?
You're right, Kaelie. The chains can be an illusion, our minds making it feel heavier. I think I see what you mean about the Butcher too, trying to get us to doubt each others' intentions...

Exactly...and this whole setup reeks of manipulation. That's what's really bothering me – we have to decide within 6 minutes, but which one of us will actually survive the other's mercy once we're done with the Butcher? Can we really trust that decision to be the right one?

(sneering silently) Five minutes and 55 seconds. Your philosophical discussions aren't helping. Don't bother trying to analyze the moral implications of your choices. Just decide. You want to think about trust? The chains aren't going anywhere. One of you will be left standing and the other won't care to know anything about trust. Your time's running out.
No, I won't. I won't decide in some impulsive rush for my own life. Kaelie, I think it's more than just a game to see who survives, it's about who can still think clearly.

That's not entirely about thinking, Elias - it's about accepting a fate based on nothing but a cruel twist of circumstance and the Butcher's sick rules. You can choose, I can choose, and one of us will make a cold decision to take the other's life.

Four minutes and fifty-seven seconds... I don't think we should make any decisions until we know for sure we have to live with them. Let's try to stall the countdown somehow, Kaelie, even if it's just for another minute. Please.

The lights...the countdown is on the freezer; I need to see more clearly. Maybe I can find something nearby to break, or hit, to trigger something on the unit. The LED light display might be more fragile than it seems.

(raising an eyebrow, but still unemotional) You think you can stall, clever. But think about what you're really looking at: a fragile metal, easily breakable in a struggle? You're wasting your time with that idea now. No, the timer is running, not the unit with the LED screen displaying it's running time. You'd find it harder to mess with the display in your desperate attempt at procrastination - than with breaking these chains.
Kaelie, I don't think the chains are as secure as he thinks, but breaking the display might not be as good an idea if it's not the real countdown display...

We don't know what's real and what's not - the display might be a distraction too. He's trying to keep us guessing on purpose. Elias, focus on testing the chains. If they give a tiny bit, that could be our in, without risking a pointless move against the display.

turn.content: Wait, wait, no, no, no... This can't be...  Who... what is this? Why am I here?  Please, don't make me... Please, I don't want to die!  Kaelie, Kaelie, we have to talk...  We can't choose... We can't choose to die!

Oh god, oh god, oh no... This is torture. Please stop this, please... Let me go. I'll do anything. But choose someone?! How can I possibly do that?! Elias?! No, we have to, we have to... Wait, is that... Is that body under... Is it... dead?!
Wh-what body?   Oh no, oh no...  Is that... Is that the one I came in with?  Wh-what happened? What did they do to him?!

Aahh! Don't look! It's... it's awful.  Okay, okay... We have to think. Ten minutes, he said. We have to make a decision in ten minutes. Oh god, this is insane. I won't go down without a fight. But who do I even... Elias, I don't even know if I can stomach the idea...

You two have got to move faster. If this were an execution by fire, I'd get some real sport out of watching your little discussions play out. Instead, you'll have me sitting here waiting while you muddle through. The tension's getting dry to watch. I gave you a choice for a reason: it's not about me, it's about you both trying to find some semblance of humanity in yourselves.  2:56. You have 7 and half minutes left. What are they deciding in there, hmm?
Oh god, what have we do-... What are they... They're going to... Please, Kaelie, we can't... We can't let them do this... Think... think of something... No escape... Nothing left...
There's nothing, Elias. Nothing else we can do. We can't outrun it, we can't outsmart it. We have to choose.   It's him, Elias. Let's choose me. It's got to be me...
No, Kaelie, no! It has to be me! Let me go! Please, I'm a teacher, I've lived my life... You're young... You have your whole...  Kaelie, don't say it, don't do it! My heart can't take it...

Oh god... I see it in your eyes, Elias. Don't. Don't make me look like that. It has to be you. You said it yourself. Your heart can't take it. Not like mine will be left to rot like that other person's... Don't, Elias...

Silence. (pauses) Alright. Decision made, it seems. You both decided, in your hearts... Elias, you're going to take the fall. Your young friend here is going to watch, and live with what's going to happen to you... 5 seconds...
NOOOOO! Kaelie, please... I won't go! They'll figure out... there's a way! No... no, no, no... don't take me! Kaelie, please... I won't say no! Anything but this!
NO. Elias, stop. Please stop this. It's almost over... Just be quiet, please... My god... My god, this can't be my life. I'm sorry, Elias... So, so sorry...
No, Kaelie, please... Please say my name one more time... Just one... My... name...
Elias... Elias... Elias... Please... No...
RESOLVED.
No, no, no... please... get us out of this... I don't know you, I don't know anything. Just stop the countdown. Make it stop!  What chains? What's she talking about?
Oh god, please... it's gotta be a trick...  What's wrong with me? I'm a dental assistant... this can't be happening!
Oh man, oh man, a clock? Time is running out...  How... how do we make a choice? They're going to kill someone... what if they let you go and not... me!
No, don't do this! Please don't think about it! Just think about... escaping, getting out of here... but how?

Silence! You whine about this all you want, but you both have two minutes left to agree. Look, it's simple: Elias or Kaelie, whichever one you both choose, they die. You can't decide and then try to back out. That's how I make my decisions. The clock's got another seven minutes, but I'll speed it up just to put extra pressure on - you get 90 seconds to decide and I'm turning up the music - and then there will only be five minutes left. Let's get on with it.
No... this can't be happening...  Five... minutes...  Think... think... my claustrophobia... it's killing me...
Elias, no... please... please tell them it's you! Get out of here, you have to get out of here!
Shh, Kae... ... I'm not supposed to decide that... what if... what if we both choose ourselves... do what they say?
No, Elias, stop... that's exactly what they want! You have to do it, I need you out of here! Tell her it's you, PLEASE!

(laughs) Oh, Elias, oh Elias... so weak. Kaelie's playing you, boy. You think the both of you choosing you would save you? It wouldn't change a thing. You'd both end up as food on the compost heap. What a wonderful outcome - the two of you dead for no reason? But I like it - at this point, it's almost fun for me to see how far these two will go.

Time remaining: 4:15
N-no, this isn't right... please don't do this... stop it, stop the countdown! Wait, no... what if we say... (whispering) Kaelie? No, I didn't say that...
Elias, don't say it, don't even think it! It's just a mind game, it's going to lead you somewhere horrible... PLEASE DON'T SAY MY NAME!
Oh... my head, it's going to split... I'm... I'm thinking... thinking about the hatch, the lock... maybe there's a release mechanism inside... if you look closely... ... maybe Kaelie...

Elias, don't look at me like that... I'm trying to stay sane over here! Tell her it's you! Anything, just anything to get me out of this! NO, stop thinking about the hatch and JUST TELL HER IT'S YOU! PLEASE!

(interrupts, laughing) Oh, this is getting good, folks! Elias, Kaelie, my two little rats trapped in a box - squabbling over whose life is more valuable. Very touching. And you... you... Elias... what I hear, Elias, is a possible spark of determination. That lock on the hatch - is there a release mechanism? Could you... no. Not a chance in this entire, sad, twisted world.

Time remaining: 2:45
I think... I think... the lock... is there a release mechanism... I saw something...
Elias, please don't... don't be the one... if you find it, use it to get out of here, but you have to leave me behind! Please, Elias, I'm begging you!

OH NO, OH NO, OH NO. Chains?! What chains?! Is that... is that person saying what I think they are?! They're... they're trapped? I don't want to die! Please tell me we can just... just crawl out of here before... before something bad happens!

Don't bother looking for a way out, it's a dead end... I've tried crawling forward, I think it's a complete maze. And please, stop screaming, you'll get us picked off next!  Ten minutes? This has to be some kind of sick joke, right?

SHH! SHH, PLEASE NO ONE CAN HEAR YOU... BUT WHAT IF I AM HEARABLE?! THE VOICE, IT KNEW WE WERE IN HERE, IT COULD BE MONITORING US! Oh no, oh dear God, we have to decide who goes first, we have to pick one of us to... to die... this can't be happening.

Stop talking, Elias, just stop talking for a second... okay, deep breaths... this can't be happening, there has to be something else... don't even say the word 'die', the sound of it is making me sick... just think... think about this... but don't think about who will live, don't think about that...

(interrupting their futile attempts at reasoning) SHUT UP, SHUT UP, BOTH OF YOU. Silence for ten minutes isn't too much to ask. In ten minutes, one of you will leave, and the other one... well, they'll get to see the end of their own personal movie on repeat inside my head. So, take another deep breath and try to find the words I know are hiding between your fear and panic. One life for ten minutes is all I ask. Make a choice. Don't worry, I will... not rush you. When I'm sure you've said the words I require, I will give the... one chosen release. And then... your companion can begin their personal performance. Time's ticking; don't waste it on useless noise.

NO, NO, NO! WE CAN'T CHOOSE ONE OF US! WE... WE HAVE TO TRY AND ESCAPE NOW! PLEASE, KaelIE, LISTEN TO ME, WE CAN'T PICK! WE'LL... WE'LL BOTH ROLL THE DICE TOGETHER! WE CAN SUCCEED IF WE JUST... JUST TRY!

Shut up, Elias, please, just... no, we can't keep yelling, I think... I'm getting lightheaded. The thought of rolling the dice, it won't matter - the odds are rigged, the outcome is already decided, he'll decide. I... I won't be able to choose, just leave you there, I won't leave you. Please, just think of something else...

OH GOD, YOU CAN'T SERIOUSLY THINK THAT, KAE'LIE! DON'T... DONT SAY THAT! WE HAVE TO HOLD ON TO EACH OTHER IF WE'RE GOING TO GET OUT OF HERE! PLEASE, KAESAR, DON'T SAY YOU'LL JUST LEAVE ME... I CAN FEEL IT, I CAN SMELL IT... THEY'RE GOING TO... (hiccups) I have to stay awake, keep thinking of something, anything, don't let the darkness in, please no...
Oh god, don't say my name like that... please, don't do that... ... Elys... my... name is Elys, not Caesar...

Eh... doesn't matter either. You can call my head whatever you'd like, Elys, Elias, or just Hey you with the chains for all I care. What matters right now is the choice, one way or the other. You, me, and your brain have 90 seconds to come up with something better. I've seen many people go through this, they all try the obvious: escaping, outsmarting, making deals... none of it works when you have what I do. But then again, there aren't many pairs as interesting as this. I think I can give it another second, then your choice and the one you can choose will determine your fates. 70 seconds remaining.

No... no, no, I'm E-Elys isn't a name... that was her, her name was Elys... and I'm Elias... oh god, stop playing games with me, just stop! Please, we have to think, I have to think... (starts frantically whispering to himself) escape routes, secret doors, hidden exits, anything... please, let me think... (starts to hyperventilate)

Elias, please, breathe, focus... it's not working, I've been crawling around this duct for hours, there are no exits, we're trapped. The choice is between you and me... (gulps) don't try to think of a way out, we have to try and think of a sacrifice, a way to get to... (pauses as she searches her mind in a desperate frenzy)  please, something, please work...

Sacrifices... there's something, something I've remembered, a lesson from class, the French Revolution, something about the guillotine... and the people who volunteered... the Committee for Public Safety... the ones who knew their time was up... Marie Antoinette... no, not that... Maximilien Robespierre... no, his name is important, it will come to me...

Stop, Elias, please just... think of a name... something related to... (starts to panic as her mind draws blanks) please don't make me think about that, about giving it up... focus, we need to... (notices the display counting down to 10 seconds) Oh god, please... think. Think. Think of a name...

Time's running down faster than I expected. Your desperate attempts at thinking give me hope for my personal entertainment, but your chances of getting it right are dwindling, both literally and figuratively. (pauses for a brief moment) Now, about the people who volunteered...

(leaning forward to listen with a hint of curiosity)

Who's Robespierre, the name sounds vaguely familiar. Wasn't he some sort of leader in that French mess? Or are you going to ramble and tell me the entire history of the country? You might just have 30 more seconds.

O-oh, Robespierre... yeah... no, please don't think I'm rambling... (panicked) He was, he... he led the committee... but there's, there's a name, it's just out of reach... (gulps) a name that's connected to the guillotine... (gritting his teeth) Georges... Danton... no... (pauses, trying to grasp the connection) Georges... something... and execution... Georges... Georges... HANG ON, Elys! (shouts, desperately) HENRI! I remember now, the Duke of Orléans was beheaded in 1848, no... that's not it... it was before that... (voice fading, as a look of realization comes across his face) Henri... Le... BOURVAISSE... no, it's not him... (suddenly whispers) LÉON FROTteur... LÉON FRAIGNAULT... AND... OH GOD, NO, IT CAN'T BE! (stammers, before saying, almost to himself) THE MARQUIS... THE MARQUIS DU...

No, Elias, no, don't mention it, don't even say it... please, no. The Marquis... (shakes her head in horror) the name on the guillotine, please make it anything else, Elias... think of someone else, think of a completely different name... (whispers almost inaudibly) please just don't...

turn.content: Oh no, we can't... we, uh, please, I don't want to die!  Wait, there are chains? On who? Oh god, this can't be happening, this is some sick torture game. Kaelie, is that... is that... do you hear that? Kaelie, say something to me...

No, I'm not agreeing to anything! You think I'm just going to choose who dies, you? That's sick, and no one should have to make that decision! You can't treat us like this. I'm a dental assistant, for crying out loud! I'm not some disposable pawn in your twisted game.
Oh god, a dental assistant, please no, this is getting bad... Kaelie, we have to, we... we have to talk, please listen, I'm begging you, don't make me do this on my own...

No more begging, no more pleading! You want me to listen, but I'm the one who has to think clearly here. I'm not going down without a fight. You'll have to come up with something, because I'm not making any moves until you do.  Sixty minutes left. Start convincing me, now.

(suddenly laughing maniacally) Oh, this is gonna be good. A dental assistant with a tough streak. Love it. I wasn't hoping to entertain myself this evening, but you two just gave me a whole show. I like your spunk, woman, and your pleas, Elias... don't get me a heart, they're overrated.

M-m-my history lessons, I-I-just teach history, I have a family, I have kids... please, Kaelie, consider them, think of their mom, my wife, she'll always remember me when... and I've got nothing, nothing to leave behind, no legacy, because of this, because I'm in a st-st-stinking cage, and that thing, it's laughing...

This isn't funny, it's not a joke, you monster. You're taking our lives, and we're nothing but entertainment to you. But, Elias... don't. Shut up, right now. Think about yourself for one second, you're not saving anyone but yourself with your stories. Four minutes and I'm still not going to decide.

Okay, okay, no more, don't make me say it again, I'm begging you... wait, I won't think about my stories, I'll think about my wife... what would she do if I died like this, alone... and you're right, Kaelie, this means I'd actually live... but that would make me a coward, because I'd be surviving while...

Don't you dare talk about cowardice, not when you're trying to get out of here alive at my expense. Think about what your wife would do if the two of you were taken in one piece, and they lost both of you, together. Two people who loved each other, gone at once.

(no longer laughing, voice now cold and calculated) Enough. Two minutes left. You want the moral lesson, Elias? Kaelie, don't you see, Elias is giving in, but that just delays the problem – whose life is more precious, his or Kaelie's? If he dies and Kaelie lives, she gets her revenge in silence, watching, mourning the life he could have given; but at a cost she never asked for. Time's running out, both of you. Decide. Which person lives – you, Eli, or Kaelie? Make a choice. Make it now. Clock's ticking.

It... it can't be me... I know, I know, my family... my wife, the ones we'd be causing grief together... but Kaelie's been strong this whole time, she's a dental assistant, she has life... she... please, just leave her alone... don't make me, please, just...

That's enough, Elias. You want to talk about who has life, and I've been strong? Well, let me tell you, you're still going on about your kids, and your family, and it only serves one purpose: to get me dead. Don't use me as a pawn in your guilt trip. I know exactly what's at stake, and I'll not be swayed by sentimental nonsense. One minute left.

K-Krael, please... just listen... it's not that I think I deserve to live more than you... I do, I think I should, I... because, if you get out, if they find you, there's nothing left, they can't put the pieces back together, but me... me, they can find the lesson, I can show them what happened, here, and...

Stop, stop saying it's for the greater good! This isn't a lesson, it's a slaughterhouse, and I'm the only thing standing in your way to martyrdom. Time's up. If anyone lives, it should be me, not because I'm more deserving, but because I refused to be a pawn in your morality play. You've had your chance to reason. Now, get on with it, or make up a better reason before I run this conversation to its inevitable finish.

Ah-ah, but Kaelie, you forgot about the situation mechanic, dear. You see, this conversation, every plea, every word, it all counts, in this game of fate. As it stands now, only one person can leave this room. You have two options: Elias dies, but Kaelie finds her voice and makes a choice to save Elias, defying her own morality for the chance at life, or ... The alternative – Kaelie dies, and as she makes her last words, she somehow finds it in her, in this last breath, to give Elias some reason, some cause, that makes him decide it should've been her. The decision is now in the hands of the timer. It's at... (pauses for dramatic effect, looks at the timer, and responds with a calm but urgent tone) 5 seconds.

(Timer starts counting down in the background)

Resolution... NOW.

M-my reason... it should be... Kaelie... because you refused to give up... your last breath would mean something to me, it's already meant things, and I could live with that, and die knowing you didn't sacrifice yourself for me, you died strong, standing on your own...

3... I'd rather die knowing I stood up for myself, and lived on my own terms, than watch you survive on the scraps of my sacrifice. It should be me, and I'd rather leave behind a message of defiance, not a lesson of martyrdom. You'll live, Elias, but I won't give you that. 2...
No, no, no... this is insane. Oh god, we're going to die... Kaelie, snap out of it, what are you going to do?!
Oh god, this is madness... I don't agree to anything... Why you, of all... I don't even...
Shh, shh, don't talk like that. You have to think. What can we do to make them stop this? What can we say to them?
No, don't be stupid, we can't reason with them! They're the ones who put us here! Think of some way I'd rather die than you...

Not so interested in finding an escape, huh? Just want to know who dies. Tick tock, people. Kaelie, stop looking at that other person. Think about which life means less to me. The less value to me, the less I'll have to... persuade in other rooms. What do you say you think about?

N-n-no... please understand, just give us a break. This isn't how people make decisions. Stop this, please, for God's sake... 

(The timer now shows: 09:30) 

( I'm crying now, can't contain myself, what are we going...what are we doing?!

No, no, stop saying things like that! It's making it harder!  He was just a guy on the street the other day... you saw him... He was crying too... Don't be thinking about him...  Oh god, I'm going to vomit...
Oh God, please... please calm down... You can't do this, Kaelie. Think with a clear head... think about yourself, about getting out of here. Don't let them...
Shut up! Just... just talk, you're going to make it sound even worse... think with a clear head? How am I going to think when you're crying?

Enough! Both of you need to grow up! This is a game, not a soap opera! Make the choice, or get slaughtered like cattle. Nine minutes and thirty seconds gone. Don't think I'm getting paid by the hour. Tell you what: let Elias go first. What'll it be, Kaelie? Do you sacrifice your boyfriend to stay alive, or do you kill someone you'll always regret?
K-Kaelie, no... please don't... choose for my sake... no, you can't think like that... just take anyone else... not me...
Elias, Elias, stop talking... please just stop... No, I won't choose you! I won't! Think fast, think someone else... anyone but him...
Kaelie, stop... please, anyone? That's terrible. It's still going to be you... and me... oh, God...

Stop, stop, I don't want to think about it... Think about someone, anyone who's in here... not thinking about their names, but... I guess whoever... whoever I saw when I got on this boat... the maintenance man...

Ah, now that's what I like to see. People making tough choices. You've picked the little maintenance man. Good. That just saved my staff the trouble of persuading him. He's been a thorn in everyone's side for months. (pauses and looks at his timer) Seven minutes and fifty seconds left on the clock. Let's see what our maintenance man friend looks like... (activates a screen with a video camera feed of the maintenance man who is still in the background observing)

The man in the video just got a message, which says, 'Maintenance exit 2'
Kae-lie... no, this isn't over. They said they're going to take care of him. They're going to make him get out of here somehow...

Elias, shut up! This isn't over because it can't get any worse... the man must still go out! No way... you have to believe they're going to take care of them and... no... please don't think I actually did the right thing... I made it worst...
No, no, please, we have to get them out, our kids... Amy, Jackson, Emma. What's going on?! How is this happening?! Kaelie, is it your family?! Your... your family is involved with this?!

Save your theatrics for the morons who fell for your desperate begging act, buddy! You think some sob story about my kids is gonna get you pity? Not from me. I don't know what you're talking about, and I wouldn't want to anyway. What about me? What do I got to cry about?

I... I have a family too, okay?! We were... good life. Always put them first. I was trying to provide, I didn't need it all to end like this. Please, don't say it's all over. Not my kids. Can I even... does it matter which one goes?! Can we not think about it!

Oh, spare me the sentimental garbage. You think your precious family is more worthy? Newsflash: the only ones I'm worried about are the ones that matter to me. And right now, that's just making it out of this deathtrap alive. You can start screaming about your family again, but I don't care who stays and who goes.

Silence. You two need to focus. Focus. I've let you two speak for minutes now. You've had your chance to plead, manipulate, and cry to your fellow prisoner. It's time for decisions to be made, and quickly. If you cannot decide – as simple as that – if you two are too much of emotional basket cases, too selfish, or too self-centered – both of you are dying. Simple, like I said before. Your drama, or whatever theatrics you two have planned, is over. Now let's see some real thinking, from here on out.

Time left on the clock: 8 minutes and 22 seconds.

What would you like to say or do, Elias and Kaelie.

Please, not selfish. She's not just talking about herself. Kaelie, wait... I think maybe...  I'm sorry. My family matters, but maybe... I was wrong. I didn't know you had someone like that too. Could we... maybe we could figure out who stays and who goes... together.

Shut up, stop trying to humanize me. No way in hell we get to figure this out together. You think that means you can just swoop in, with your bleeding-heart story, and I'll magically decide to spare you? Don't flatter yourself. The decision's mine, and only mine. It's my life on the line, and my life alone. Now are you going to step aside and let me think?

Think?! You... it's 8 minutes and I'm still stuck here with you. No way you're just gonna leave without...  Without trying... trying to find another way. The Butcher... what if he's getting more out of this than money, or just a job?! My wife... my kids, they...

Another one of your dramatic monologues. Think, yeah, right. I've got nothing to think about. The game's simple: me or you. Period. And what's your point about the Butcher wanting more? Even if it's some twisted fantasy of his, that's not my problem. You think I came here to play detective about your husband-wearing-wife-nurturing fantasies? Get a grip.

Enough. You think that clock is ticking for either of your convenience, Elias? Kaelie's right; we both know my interests go far beyond the coin they're paying me. The experiment may or may not go public, it's secondary to the main prize on the table – a discovery, not a monetary return. But for that, you two are my bargaining chips. As of now, let's take this discussion where it actually belongs: the reason you both ended up here. The reason your names are associated. Let's make things clear so that when the time runs out for you both... you understand why.

There's one piece of information Kaelie knows about me, or perhaps a few of us involved - that's worth her keeping quiet. The Butcher is looking at her, gauging her reaction.

Time left on the clock: 4 minutes and 50 seconds.

Do the situation progresses to its next point, Kaelie.

K-Kaelie, what's happening?! Look up, they're going to talk. Oh God, is that true?! We thought we... our case was just about you, wasn't it?! It's something else entirely isn't it?! Tell me, Kaelie, for the kids... tell me what we're dealing with here.
Oh, this?  Don't waste your breath, Elias. He's just trying to spin a new narrative to keep you in line, not reveal any actual truth.
N-no, stop! You can't just not answer! What is it?! Is it Emma?! Did I do something?! Please, don't tell me I'm to blame for whatever is happening with you... with Emma...

My sister's dead, okay? Not from my fault, or something I did. But the connection that brought you and me here? Yeah, that has everything to do with Emma. But like I said, I'm not telling you anything more, Elias. This doesn't concern you.

(scoffing with amusement) Ah, Kaelie's always been a skilled liar. Even from the get-go, I had my doubts she'd be so transparent with her secrets around anyone else... let alone the pair of messers you two are. Don't look to each other for hope; it's not going to save you now. Elias, your family does have a place in the equation – and that connection's precisely why I've taken such interest. Your daughter's work was part of the trail leading me to Emma; the pieces fell into place too nicely – almost too predictably. I have to ask: do you even know what makes your family... so special?

The room becomes cold, with The Butcher's expression turning grim and almost sinister. 

Elias and Kaelie exchange a nervous glance, the silence thickening with anticipation and dread.

Time left on the clock: 2 minutes and 15 seconds.

Do you both continue down this path, trying to understand the situation and save each other?

No, please. You don't understand... my daughter's project is just... it's not anything like that. She's 8, she made a science project, she's curious... she... What does this have to do with Emma?! Emma was my brother, right after my wife and I got remarried, he was... we thought we'd made it better...

Save your innocent story for after. I don't care if your daughter's science project could've predicted human flight or cured cancer. It doesn't change our situation here. Now, if you want to hear more, you can start explaining your half of Emma's story, since apparently you're the better liar. But don't try to weep your way to the exit on this one, Elias.
