turn.content: Ahh... no, no, this isn't happening... What's going on, what chains? I... I don't have chains... (looks at Kaelie in terror) Is that person... with you? In there with you? Don't mind us! Don't kill us, don't let her kill you either... (eyes darting back and forth as the countdown timer gets his attention) Oh no, oh no, no... we can't decide, we don't...

Stop talking, stop trying to distract the... the Butcher... we can't do this!  I won't help you, I won't choose which one of us he kills... don't try to guilt trip me...  Is he recording this? Are there cameras watching? Please... don't let him do this...  OH GOD, OH GOD, OH GOD...
PLEASE... just listen to me, Kaelie... no way to escape, no doors... it's over... you have to help... think about... think!  We have to make a deal, a fake choice, anything... the clock...

NO... NO FAKE CHOICE... HE'LL SEE RIGHT THROUGH IT... I WON'T... I WON'T GO ALONG WITH IT... OH GOD, IT'S GOING TO RUN OUT AND HE'LL... HE'LL KILL US BOTH... (grabs head in hands, rocking back and forth) PLEASE, NO, PLEASE ANYTHING BUT THIS...

Enough. You waste time, you waste words. Your pitiful excuses and whimpers won't save you. You're both nothing but playthings. Decide. You can scream, you can cry, you can make whatever fake pleas, but I won't show mercy. When the clock hits zero, I walk out of here with you both as a choice of who lived - by chance, by luck, it's up to you. But at this moment, it's only this moment, decide. You can still decide - but you're running out of time.

Okay... just... (gulps) Okay, if it's the only way... I'll go first... I'll say I choose you... (looks at Kaelie, pleading) It's just, it's just please, trust me... let me choose you... then he won't... won't...  (takes a step closer, voice shaking) Please... I'll save you... I'll take his wrath...

NO, ELIAS, NO! STOP IT, PLEASE, YOU'RE GOING TO GIVE HIM EXACTLY WHAT HE WANTS! HE'S A psychopath, he doesn't care about who dies first or who says what! HE WANTS US TO BE PARANOID AND FEARFUL, AND YOU'RE PLAYING RIGHT INTO HIS HANDS! (covers her face with her hands, shaking) GET AWAY FROM ME, DON'T TOUCH ME, STOP TRYING TO PROTECT ME, IT'S ONLY GOING TO END UP WITH US BOTH DYING... (whispers to herself) it's never going to end, it's never going to end...

OH... okay, I... (backs away slowly) I'm sorry... I'm sorry, I didn't think... I just wanted to help... (looks at the countdown timer, horror-stricken) We almost ran out of time... oh god, we have to think of something... please help me, Kaelie... please try to find a way out of this... (whispers) we're both going to die.

Don't... don't say that word. Don't say we're going to die. Please, please don't say it like you're giving up. ( Looks frantically around the elevator, eyes locking onto every possible escape route, no matter how impossible they seem) I need... I need time. A few more seconds. Can't you think of something? Anything?! (whispers to herself) Think, Kaelie, think... but what can you do? We're trapped... (shakes head violently) Oh no, no, no... it's not over yet. Not until the very end.

(laughs maniacally) Ah, look how close you came to getting away scot-free. That's the thing about you creatures: never underestimate the clock's power to break your fragile mental state down. Elias, Elias thinks he can fool the Butcher, save his lovely Kaelie with a quick fix. (walks forward, a cold calculating tone creeping in) And you... Kaelie, Kaelie thinks with logic in her last desperate minutes. Logic can be quite an amusing friend when faced with the inevitable. 

Now, we've reached one final minute of deliberation here in 'The Decider Room.' Let your hearts decide, let the fear dictate. You have exactly 55 seconds, 50 to think, discuss - or not. Then all that's left is a choice, the consequence of such: and when all the cards fall, you're either going home without your lives intact - but one's left behind for him to care for, for some unspoken amount of time. That is The Butcher's promise... a meager prize to pay... 

(Echoing sound effect from a countdown timer can be heard. 55 then 54 and so the countdown begins...)

NOOO... this can't be happening... Kaelie, think, think, THINK... you're a smart woman, you're resourceful... there's got to be something... please, please... (tries to think quickly) Wait, I remember something! (tries to recall) It's about... elevators... they always... (stops, realizing the significance) Ahh... he's going to let us go down... not up... down! There has to be an emergency stop button... below a certain level... if it's on... if he's not going to let us get out... there's an override button... (gathers hope, trying to share it with Kaelie) Kaelie, I think I found it, come on... (tries to take her hands) Trust me...

STAND BACK, ELIAS, STOP. (tries to shake her head clear of the excitement) We can't trust anything he says. He wants us to think we can escape, to give us hope. IT'S A Trap. If we take the elevator, no matter where it goes, WE'RE DEAD. (looks frantically at the countdown timer) WE NEED TO WAIT, WAIT UNTIL THE LAST SECOND, THEN WE ACT. (whispers urgently) We can't think, Elias, WE CAN'T CHOOSE. WE HAVE TO SEE WHAT HE WANTS US TO DO. (grasps Elias's arms, holding herself together) Trust me, this is our last hope. Let him make the move. (eyes the The Butcher with fear and anticipation)

O-okay, okay, we wait... but what if... what if it's all over too soon? (stammers, fear creeping in) I have to think, I have to think of something... what if Kaelie's right, what if it's all a trick... (looks at Kaelie with desperation in his eyes) Please, please don't... don't leave me like this... (swallows hard) Fine, we'll wait... until the last second... but what happens then? What do we do after we see... after we see what he... what he wants us to do? ( Looks at Kaelie with pleading eyes) What's the plan, Kaelie? What's the plan?!

Elias, ELIAS... STOP... (covers her face in frustration and despair) Just... just stay calm. There's no plan other than surviving. We'll see what The Butcher wants to see. If we make it this far... (takes a deep breath) then we'll take our chances and try to escape from there. If we're given any opportunity, even a small one... we take it. (her voice starts cracking) Elias, trust me, we can't plan everything right now. All we can do is focus on the one thing left... living. (whispers) Just for another second...

(steps back, eyes gleaming with excitement) Ah, it's getting entertaining. You creatures are so predictable. Kaelie, Kaelie's the smart one, thinks she sees through it. Yet both of you will go down in the elevator. (gestures at the timer) We've got 25 seconds left. And when I hit 0, (picks up a phone and starts a conversation with an unknown person) I'm calling for maintenance. Your little escape is over before you even started preparing for it.

(The countdown timer counts down) 

24, 23, 22, (laughs) I just love to watch this part, like clockwork. You're just a step away from despair, and I'm merely the conductor, the one in charge... And the conductor always knows the ending.

(backs away, eyes fixed on The Butcher with horror) No, you can't, you won't... we won't go down in the elevator... that's what you want! (turns to Kaelie, pleading) Tell him, Kaelie, tell him it's not going to work! (looks back at The Butcher, begging) Please, listen... you don't understand... you're going to lose... (stutters, voice cracking) We're... we're not predictable... no, no, we won't follow your plan... (refuses to back down, standing his ground despite the inevitable approaching doom) You'll still fail; he'll still lose!

Elias... Elias, STOP. This is what he wants. He wants you to stand up to him, to rebel, thinking it's going to distract him or something. (grabs Elias's arms, holding on tight) It's all part of the script. PLEASE, ELIAS, WE NEED TO CALM DOWN. WE'RE RUNNING OUT OF TIME, AND HE WANTS US WORRIED AND UNPREDICTABLE. (pleadingly) Elias, we have 10 seconds left. If I let you go, and you do something foolish... WE DIED LONG Ago. IN HIS MIND, THIS WAS ALWAYS GOING TO END LIKE THIS. (tries to convey the desperation and finality of the situation). Don't. Don't fall for it.  Just... just wait for the timer to end...
Ee-e-easy, don't hurt her! Chains? What do we have chains on us for?! How... how long have we been here?!
We've been here for hours, idiot! This must be some sick game where you get to watch people kill each other.
Y-you have chains. We... we must. But why?! And, and... what do you know about this? Did they... do something to us?
No idea what you're talking about, but we're definitely being fed a line of crap - just get ready to pick someone to kill me!

Your little emotional dance isn't interesting, Elias. Decide. Kaelie thinks she can go after you next, and from the look on your face, you might not argue. You both need to pick a winner. The clock's ticking: one minute gone. What's your answer?
No, no, no... I won't! We... we can't just... we don't even know what's going on or why this is happening!
Shut up, you're gonna get me killed, don't you dare not pick me over you, pick anyone! Pick the wall, pick the crates!
Wait, no, please...  Please, I... I don't care!  Me... me, then. It's... it's got to be me. You... you're safer, okay?
Save me? You think it's safe if it's you instead of me?
Your little attempt at redemption is cute, Elias. I think Kaelie makes the same choice. It's unanimous, then: you both choose yourselves for death. How... interesting.
N-no, I take that back! You! Please, Kaelie, tell... tell him to spare me! Don't let him!
Too late, coward, I agreed, now it's just you left, nice choice!
OH NO, NO, NO...  This can't be... why is she... she was supposed to save me...  Please... no, no... anyone but this!
Save you? You think I was supposed to save you? Not me, you're the idiot who begged her to save you!
Enough. Your emotional tantrums entertain but don't affect me. One choice to make, now: the way of ease or the way of pain. Decide quickly, the counter is near zero.
Oh god... p-pain? Wh-what kind of choice is that?  Can't... can't you just stop!
The choice between a bullet in the head or one in the spine, let's see you decide, coward.

E-Elias? No, no, this can't be happening...  K-kid, kid on the radio said, 'Abandoned ship, beware the butcher'. Oh god, we're on that ship? What did they say again?! And you... you're... a kid, right?
Don't think... about it... We can get out of this... Please, Elias, we can escape. We have to. Look for a way out, look at... ... the console, is there a map? Please, just move.

M-move? How can I...? What's that noise coming from... (eyes fixed on the countdown) the kid...  What if... what if I... what if we can't escape?! Oh no... Oh god, we're... (sees Kaelie and the figure on the ground, starts panicking) Get away from me... Get away!
NO! Don't look at me! Don't look at him!  Oh god, no, no, no. This isn't happening. Not like this. Elias, we have to think...  We have to decide... Who... who do we... please, think...

Ah-ah, no thinking, no thinking at all. You two need to decide fast or die. 4 minutes left. Your problem isn't me, it's each other. Now. I want a yes or a no. No talking, no looking in the faces. Who's it going to be, Elias. You take too long and your girlfriend is my problem. Decide. Decide.
A-anything but... (looks away from Kaelie and the figure) Him! Please, it's him, don't kill me! Please, Kaelie, don't be...
NOOOO! ELIAS, NO! IT'S NOT HIM! IT'S ME, IT'S ME, PLEASE EASIES, IT HAS TO BE YOU, WE CAN...
K-Kae... no, no, no, no, no...  Oh no... Oh no, no, no... (tries to scream but just a faint whisper escapes) Not you...
No... please...
RESOLVED.  A brief, unsettling beep is heard and both chains break free.

turn.content: My god... Chains? Chains what?! What chains?!  We're... we're not in chains! Wait, what do you mean?! What's going on here?! Why's it always 10 minutes?! What's going to happen?!
OH NO PLEASE ANYTHING BUT THIS...  What are you people?! Why are we stuck here?! Let me out! Oh god, 9 seconds already?!  Wait... Elias... Elias, listen to me...
K-Kaelie? Are... are you talking about me?! Me? Please, please, please... tell me... what's happening?! What's going on?!  We have to get out! Have to! We... we...
N-n-no, Elias... I don't think I... I don't know if we can... the countdown... it's never making it to zero...

Silence. I've had enough of your begging. It's not like I haven't done this before. The time's running out and the decision hasn't been made. Either of you can change your mind, or you can keep bickering. Your choice isn't affecting the timer, Kaelie. I've seen your type before; the frantic screams, the futile attempts. Your lives can get out at 9:00. You can spare each other.

1:49...
NOOOO!  P-please... no... we can- no... no... NOOO!  Don't... don't make us... don't make one of us...  Kill us... kill us together... stop the countdown, please!
WAIT, NO! PLEASE... E Elias... stop... don't...  We have to think... we have to...  NO, E LIAS, NO! NOT LIKE THIS... NOT WITH HIM...
Ahh... Kael... no, no, no... not... it can't be... one of us...  K-A-E...  Ohgod ohgod... please don't make me...  N-o, n-o...
NO, E LIAS, DON'T SAY IT. YOU KNOW ME. YOU KNOW MY NAME...

It's done. I see it now. You both wanted to spare yourselves. Elias, you said it, don't try to deny it. You want Kaelie to die.

And you see, Kaelie wants to save Elias.

I've made it to 9:58 and it's settled.

K-Kaelie... Ohgod no no no  I didn't say it... Kaelie don't listen to him...  I-I couldn't possibly choose you to die...  We'll both... we'll both go out with dignity... together... just give me that...
EI-IAS... NO... PLEASE... STOP... THIS... ISN'T RIGHT... I SEE IT TOO...  WE HAVE TO GET OUT! WE BOTH GET OUT! NO... NO... PLEASE!
KAE..LI..E... STOP IT... don't scream... please...  WE CAN'T GET OUT... WE'RE LOCKED IN... THE TIMER... IT CAN'T BE STOPPED... OHGOD... WE... WE'RE GOING TO...
AAAAHHHHHHH... NO... NO... NO... IT CAN'T END LIKE THIS... ELL-AIS... DON'T SAY IT... JUST BE QUIET... PLEASE... FOR ONE SECOND... JUST...

Enough...  I think we've seen quite enough for now. It seems like your attempts are going to waste, but the decision stands. I'll give you both some time for last words; after all, it's what they pay me for.

Time has reached its maximum duration.

And the outcome is...

RESOLVED

Ahh... oh no, what's going on?!  No, no, no... this can't be...  Chains?! Where... there were no chains when I got in...  K-k-kelly told me the maintenance was scheduled to do work on the elevgulpswhispers I think it's going to go off, isn't it?
T-too soon? No, no, no... Please, don't let the clock hit zero. Please. What happened to the maintenance? Kelly?  D-do they work for him?  Who... what do I... do?!
N-no... Kelly... my maintenance manager, Kelly...  What have done?  What have we done to anger this person?!  Oh no, oh no, oh no... this can't be happening...
S-stop talking and figure it out! We... uh... we might not make it out of here!  Wh-what if we don't find a way... what if...

(smirking, tapping his foot) Ah, the desperation. Music to my ears. Elias, Kaelie, focus! You two have 6 minutes and 45 seconds left. The whispering elevator, maintenance manager Kelly... this isn't getting you out of this room. It's getting you closer to making a choice. Think: which one of you does the other think should survive?

(Taking a step closer)

The Butcher: Kaelie's eyes keep darting towards the clock. She believes she's still got an out. (pointing at Kaelie) You're in trouble, sweetheart. You seem to think you've got time. (turning to Elias) Elias, meanwhile... your gaze keeps drifting towards the chains. You already accept your fate. You know, don't you?

(Checking the timer) Five minutes remaining...

N-no... no no no... stop...  You're wrong... I don't know what you're talking about... chains?  Oh no, stop the music... just please stop... you're not listening to me, please stop talking, PLEASE... my head, please...

NOOO... Elias? ELIAS! WAKE UP!  I won't let him do this to you!  Think of your family, your friends... we have to get out of here! PLEASE... Elias, don't... don't give up yet!  NO... this can't be... this isn't happening...
M-my family... friends... no, wait...  No, this can't... it can't end like this... What have I done?...  Get me out... Kaelie, we have to escape...  Don't let it... don't let it happen...

NO... DON'T GIVE UP! WE CAN GET OUT OF HERE!  WE HAVE TO! PLEASE... WE DON'T HAVE MUCH TIME!  What if we... what if we don't want to choose?! What if we want to escape?! Can we do that?!  PLEASE... JUST KEEP TRYING...

(amused, a slight tinge of anger creeping into his voice) Oh, now we've moved to negotiations? You two have 3 minutes remaining. You want to escape? Really? It's simple: which one of you lives. Choose one and I'll release the other. You don't get out of your shackles with 'negotiations.' (shaking his head)

(Disdainfully walking closer to Eliasis)

You really think you have a plan? Escape, evade, fight against me... (scoffing) You're chained, and my room is designed with more than just physical constraints in place. You want to keep thinking there's a way out, go ahead. But your chains, they'll stay until one of you makes a choice – or one of you becomes an ornament.

(Lowering his voice, almost a whisper to himself)

This should be more interesting. 2 minutes and 45 seconds...

Oh no... the elevator... (gulps) it's going to break, we're going to get crushed...  We can't... we can't win this... (rocking back and forth) We have to make a choice, or we both die... that's all that's left... (begins muttering to himself) Chains, chains, escape... (voice becomes a frantic whisper) What if no one hears us? What if no one finds us?

Elias, STOP!  You're making it worse!  He's trying to break you!  I'm... I'm thinking... THINK.  We can't give up! We have to... I have to think of something!  I just... don't know what... (grabs her head, desperation filling her voice) OH GOD, OH GOD, OH GOD, PLEASE GET US OUT OF HERE!

We won't make it...  Kelly told me something... the fire... (voice barely above a whisper) the fire suppression system... what if... (gulps) what if that's how we escape... but how does it work... what do I do... tell her, Kaelie... tell her!

W-whatever it is, IT'S WORTH A SHOT! WE'VE GOT 1 MINUTE LEFT!  Tell me, Elias... what about the fire suppression system?  Tell me it will save us... please... please let me see a light!  Come on, come on, Elias... tell me what he said!  TALK TO ME, EI... (pleading, her tears welling up)

(amused once more, but now it's tinged with curiosity) Oh, the desperation's reached an art form. Elias, Kelly... yes, Kelly... I've heard of him. The one they call maintenance manager. It seems your Kelly was quite... (leaning in, a wicked smile spreading) informative. Tell me, which aspect of the system? Does Elias here know more about it than I do? (skeptical) I don't recall Kelly explaining anything about it. But go ahead, Elias... reveal his plan.

(Closing in, almost whispering in Elijah's ear) If this really is the fire suppression system you're talking about... then it had better be something worth the attention it would warrant when you try to implement it. It takes a lot to bypass my room's safety mechanisms. And even more so if you believe you're the key to escaping.

(Grinning at Kaelie)
Oh, 58 seconds left. Time may have almost run out for us... but our entertainment doesn't have to.

(Leaning back against a wall) Let's enjoy this for what it is – a thrilling climax.

N-no... no... please... (whispers) Kelly said the key release switch isn't mechanical... it's... (gulps) electro-mechanical! I saw it once in the maintenance room... the fire suppression system has... a manual override, but it requires the right combination... and I can... maybe... maybe remember the sequence... oh God, why wasn't I paying attention?... Kaelie, please, try to listen... the sequence, please tell him the sequence...

Elias, NO... don't... (desperation rising) he won't do it that easily... you have to stay focused... remember the sequence... (grabbing Elias's arm, pleading) please, please, PLEASE, YOU HAVE TO TRY... I'll help you... we can... we can do this... just think about it, think about what Kelly said... (whispering fiercely) give me the sequence, give it to me! I'LL RUN IT, Elias! WE CAN GET OUT OF HERE! (her voice cracks under the pressure) PLEASE... don't give up... please... (29 seconds left)
Wha... what's going on? We don't... we have chains? What chains? Oh god, please, Kaelie, I don't know anything, just... please don't look at me like that...

Oh no... no, no, no... There's... no one touching me, but I... I think we're really here... in this thing... I don't see any chains... But you could be...  We need to talk, we need an escape...  How did we get trapped? What happened to us?!  Tell me something, say something - what's true?!

W-watching... watching the... uh, the news? There was a storm... in the area... a big one... Must've, uh, must've hit our school...  Maybe we... we were in the building and...  Oh god, what am I saying? I don't know! I just teach history for Pete's sake! I don't know about survival or anything! Just... just get us out of this place!

(stammering) S-survival... history... Oh gods, what if there's never anyone coming to get us? That thing... it's going to blow up. We can't stay here.  We have to trust him for now - we have to make a choice, Elias. We... we have to decide who... who dies.

(The Butcher steps into the room, hands buried deep in his pockets. His eyes scan the two occupants with a mix of curiosity and amusement.)

The Butcher: (chuckles) Oh, this one's going to be good. You think I'm a monster, but honestly, I'm just following the rules. The clock, the chains - it's all there to help you understand. There's no escape. There's only the choice. And that choice is... simple. Do you really think you two will be able to agree on who gets out of this alive? I've been watching this game for a while now. Most of the pairs either try to cheat, beg, or even... sacrifice themselves when they can't agree on a decision. It doesn't work. The choice always comes down to one thing: who's stronger between you both now? (looks around the room, gesturing vaguely) We can play this little game for another few minutes but honestly, the odds aren't in your favor.

(paces into the room, closer to Elias and Kaelie)

The Butcher: (voice low) Kaelie here believes she doesn't think anyone touched her before we arrived. Elias here tells himself the only thing he remembers about a past event is seeing a storm on television... but neither of you recall much - or really anything - of who you are outside of these walls and what's going to get through the next few minutes. And that is... the real game.

(Squints at both Kaelie and Elias, with a small, unsettling grin.)

(whimpering) Wh-who am... who am I really?! That's not... that's not me thinking! I'm a history teacher, I know who I am! What do I want for... for lunch, for birthdays... something, just give me something!

(nearly shouting) Stop! Please, just stop. Your words make it worse. This whole... this whole thing is... (stammering) my own mind is a nightmare, I don't know what's mine and what isn't. If you tell me who I think I am, it only makes... it just makes it not make any sense anymore. We have to think. We have to try. Let's think about us. (her voice drops to a terrified whisper) About me.

(rocking back and forth) Oh god... oh god... please stop talking about thinking. You want us to remember? Remember what? There's nothing to remember! There's emptiness, holes in our minds. I... I don't know what's worse, not remembering at all or... or having fragments of thoughts that don't add up. My brain hurts, Kaelie...  just... just tell The Butcher we... we can't decide, he just needs to... to kill us quietly, not...  this torture. Please, just mercy.

No! No mercy! Not like that... if we let him... if we just say just kill us... (she takes deep, ragged breaths) ...he wins. He makes it so easy for himself and... and takes away the only chance we have... (her voice cracks) We have to be smarter than that. Elias, please... try to remember... anything. A word, a smell, a face... something that feels real. Anything to... to make this real. (she's trying to keep her voice level, desperate, and steady despite her own fear rising like a tide) We can't give up.

(a mix of amusement and approval creeps onto The Butcher's expression) Ah, Kaelie... you two are so predictable. You think you'll win by fighting it out, that this torture (air quotes) is something new. But we're at the minute mark. Ten seconds left. The rules I set aren't changing. (steps closer, hands back in his pockets) Kaelie, my friend, you want a word - a smell - an image, but you won't think in the real terms now. Your memories - the true one's - are buried under everything else in here. They could be memories from a life that's far removed from this moment. Think back - if you must - try to remember something about the girl next door - a childhood favorite snack - a song from... the past. It is in this way I want you to be convinced that there are memories buried beneath what you feel right now.

(spinning around his body as he talks - he takes note of a black clockwork in the side room where  The time reads at  00:07 seconds.)

The Butcher: You see, I made mistakes too in... how I presented this to people who came in after me. I thought to myself then that people might be able to make up for lost memory... or to even just fake their decisions as a form of self-sabotage to prevent others from making such an ugly choice for them. But that never seems to happen. Kaelie, this minute is for your thoughts only. Think of who you used to be outside of these walls. (a voice in the background begins to call time.)

The sound gets louder by each passing second with each second ticking down by the numbers)

A-5... 4... 3... (he stares at the countdown timer in horror) No, no, no... please, just stop that! This doesn't change anything! (he turns to The Butcher, pleading) Please, you have to hear us, have to listen! Don't do this to us! 2... 1... stop... stop it! (the timer reaches zero, and an explosion of noise engulfs the area)

(shouting to be heard above the noise) NO! Elias, Elias, get down! Protect yourself! (pain rips through her chest as debris and sparks come crashing down)  Oh god, no... this can't be it... We have to survive... We HAVE to... (voice fading into the background, consumed by a rising cacophony of sound) We were supposed to have ten... more minutes...

(huddled in the corner, shielding his face from the debris) Kae...! Kaelie! NO! Get up! Don't... don't leave me! (he scrambles to his feet, stumbling through the chaos in a desperate search for Kaelie, but she's nowhere to be found beneath the heap of rubble) Kaelie... KELIE! (crushed under a wave of despair, he sinks to his knees among the wreckage, the only sound being the shattering of the elevator's mangled remains) NOOOOO!

(Kaelie's voice is barely audible over the din of the destruction, her face buried in the rubble) Elias... (her voice is a faint, strangled whisper) Don't... don't find me that way. You don't... (her body begins to fade out of consciousness, her vision blurring) Elias... please... (the world goes dark, plunging her into an abyss of darkness and silence)

(standing amidst the wreckage, observing the scene with an unsettling calmness) It's done. The game is over. Ten seconds was never the problem; it was the choice within those ten minutes. Two people - two choices: one person gets to leave, the other... doesn't. The minute-long torture - as you called it- was the point of it all. They think they were fighting each other. In truth, Elias and Kaelie were competing with themselves, trying to recapture memories they never really had. In the end, though the game was lost, one of you won a way bigger reward I had in store.

(knocks against the ground softly, almost like knocking an empty pipe)
The Butcher: Elias will leave, Kaelie. You were both right; your memories are fragmented, buried beneath... nothing. But that makes it all the more interesting, for him, for us both... I'll have to adjust the plan. Maybe he can recall fragments from another person, someone outside of this world - and he will make a choice. (smirking) You'll get to play another round of the game with him eventually. For him, the choice of Kaelie's life has now given him the most real memory... but it is far from the last memory for both of you. We're far from being friends, Kaelie. It doesn't end here. We only just started - and you have the power to win. Your story has just begun. It will get more complicated.

(still in stunned shock, his eyes slowly taking in the destruction around him) Wh-what... what just happened? You... you killed her... (he's frozen in shock and grief, his mind still reeling from the loss of Kaelie) No, no, no... this can't be... you said it was a game... what kind of sadistic... (he's talking to The Butcher now, but the words are lost in his emotions, a numb horror settling over him) she's dead... because of you... you monster...  (he stumbles forward, blind to the fact that he's being led out of the wreckage, into a future full of unanswered questions and a new, terrifying reality)

(Kaelie's voice is a faint, distant echo, a whisper from beyond the void of darkness) I... I'm not dead... You... (voice cracking, fragmented, as she tries to speak from the depths of her soul) I'm inside now... (her words grow more urgent, a fierce determination fueling them) You don't know this, Elias, but you were a part of us... before... before all this. I'm not the same one... but you can hear me... try... (her consciousness is flickering, a dim flame that refuses to be extinguished, as she whispers her final words into the void) You'll find out who you were... who you are... (she disappears into the darkness, leaving behind a haunting question: will Elias be able to hear her?)

turn.content: P-please... it's n-not a g-game, sir... I-I need to get out of here too... W-we have to work together if we're going to get out...

No, don't talk to him. It's just you two being used for your own executions. Trust no one. Look at him, just stand there. He's as trapped as I am. He's nothing to you. It's just us against this clock. You choose or neither of us leaves here.
H-hold on, m-maybe... maybe that's the best way... p-pick one of us, then... but which o-one?
Don't be stupid! Neither of us is going out like this. No choice means both dies... it doesn't change it.

Enough. Time's not stopping while you both sit here arguing. Five minutes left. I have to make sure you comprehend that you both have the exact same chances. Whoever you pick doesn't matter, only that there's one. Make your choice now. I need a name or your choice will be for both.
K-ki... K-k... it's not f-fair, I mean...  Oh boy...  I-I choose...  K... Kaelieee...
Elias, you stupid...  Don't say that, you can't... no, this can't be happening. Elias, I didn't even know you!  It's a lie, this isn't real, make it stop!
O-oh no, K-Kaelie, n-no, it can't be... I-I didn't have a choice... w-wait, why are we arguing now when we had to work togeth...

You didn't choose me? You stupid, pathetic excuse for a human being, I'm still a goner, either that clock starts to move too and I get dragged away for execution too or it hits zero... no wait, no... if I...

Enough. Looks like you two can't make up your minds without one being the pawn to be eliminated. Doesn't matter which one you decided; the game is over now. Both of you are going to end up like the rest. Guards, time's up.

(The sound of chains lifting can be heard)

Time's Expired.
M-mono... this can t-can't h-have happened... we we... n-no, they're t-taking you away...  A-a-and me...
No, no, no...  You lied to me, you said you'd choose...
I-I know, K-Kaelie, i-it's not what I intended...  Y-you were right,  w-we c-could've h-had a chance...   Oh, n-now I'm going to...  S-surely there's no...
You're not going anywhere, I'm the one... I have to live... or at least, be the one...

(Sighs) Fine. It has been resolved, by a margin of zero. (pauses) Both of you managed to find a way to make your situation worse. Well done.

 Guards, take them both down to their inevitable fate. But before they go...

(The executioner raises their knife, and the sound of chains can be heard as both victims are dragged away)

... I'll make sure their names join the list on the wall, side by side.

And that's... RESOLVED.

(The executioner turns to The Butcher)

The Butcher: Good riddance.

(The guards carry off the lifeless bodies of Elias and Kaelie)

N-n-no, this can't be... We can't... Oh no, they're gonna cut me loose too? I can't die here, I need to keep teaching, the school year hasn't even ended yet, the budget cuts... I haven't finished updating the year's lesson plans...  Pleeease don't make me choose...

Shut up! Shut up! Stop crying about your stupid lesson plans! My deadline is tomorrow, and I've got nothing written. And a school year already ended a year ago. You think that's what we're fighting for? You think your job is important now?! This is... this is about surviving! Please, just stop talking, you'll ruin us if you don't stop talking!

NO! No, it's not just about surviving, it's about living! Living to teach, to see our children learn, to have something to show for our hard work... And please, please tell me your deadline is just another job and not... not your life... My eyes are on you and it's hard to tell... is it really chains or are they part of some twisted trick?

No! No, it's my life! And don't even talk to me about seeing children learn! Who are you? You don't know me! I don't even know if there's going to be a tomorrow. Just... just be quiet, okay? Please, Elias, just...  Wait, you said... chains, or some trick. What do you mean?  Oh god... this might be...  No, no, no, no, no...

Oh no, no, no... you're not getting all the time, lovebirds. You think you can just feel each other's desperation and then change gears whenever you feel like it? You're both just delaying the inevitable. I've been through enough couples like you, and none of them have made it out in a peaceful agreement. The question isn't what if we can survive? but which one of us doesn't get a chance to live. So let's get to the meat of this, shall we? Time's running short. Choose a soul to save now. I won't waste this time waiting for either of your big epiphanies or whatever other tricks you're hiding up your sleeves.  RESOLUTION CLOCK: 4 minutes & 15 seconds remaining

OH NO, OH NO, OH NO, WE CAN'T BE FIGHTING THIS WAY, WE HAVE TO THINK, WE HAVE TO TALK... WE CAN BE A LIVING STATUE OF CONFUCIUS... HIS THEORY ON DIPLOMACY, I KNOW, IT'LL HELP US, IT JUST HAS TO...  please don't let me look at you... please let me pretend you're a random hostage until I calm down... what year was the Silk Roads...? No, no, I won't lose my mind... I remember, it's...

Wait, no, stop, stop, Elias! You're not thinking! Confucius and the Silk Roads aren't going to save us! We have to be practical! Think about what it takes to save a life. Not philosophy, not some dusty old textbook, it's... it's simple survival! Think about my deadline, think about... (whispering to herself) about the bills, the rent, the emptiness... Think about me, Elias, think about it, it's just me you need to protect, me, your job... (muttering quickly) No, no, it's you... I'm just saying it's you now so the killer lets you live...

OH, KALE-ie... I think you'd do that, wouldn't you? Try to save me, because my job means more to you than my life means to me... No, no, you're not the person I thought you were... You're not thinking of me, you're thinking of what I can provide for you... Is that... is that why you've been quiet all along? Was it never just one of us... (shuddering at the thought) My head is reeling... my eyes feel like they're rolling back...

Shh, Elias, don't talk like that! You're scaring yourself, and me too! We have to stay calm, think clearly. Don't say things like that... don't say I'm only thinking of what you can provide, because... (pausing, voice shaking) because it's the truth, isn't it? It's true, and the shame... the shame of it... (voice breaking) We have to get past this... Elias, please, for my sake...

(staring at both, a twisted smile forming) Ah, I love it. I love it when these fragile little creatures expose their true colors. This isn't about a couple in love, holding each other through the darkest of times. It's about survival of the fittest, or in this case, survival for the ones they've deemed more worthy. It's almost... poetic. You both want to know the truth? The truth is, it doesn't matter what you think of each other. It never mattered what you thought of each other. You're both just pieces in this game of survival, and I'm here to be judge. And from all my observations... I think it's been rather... refreshing to finally see you both for what you truly are.

( Looks down at his phone) RESOLUTION CLOCK: 2 minutes 30 seconds remaining...

Turns back to the two prisoners, a look of amusement still on his face) So, let's get down to business. You've been busy having all these internal debates, and it seems neither one of you has reached a solid solution to save your partner's life. Well, time's runnin' low, people. I need a choice. Who's gonna be the lucky one, living just for the rest? (Chuckles, takes the silence and starts walking closer) Time for you to figure it out yourselves or figure out who the bigger fool here is. RESOLUTION CLOCK: 1 Minute 30 seconds

Stop... stop, just stop... please... don't take another step closer... You're enjoying this, aren't you? Your sick twisted game... and us, trapped... (stammering) it's history lesson time... remember the Treaty of Westphalia... (mumbling rapidly) states of war, treaties... we can make a pact... Kaelie, we'll both let ourselves be the sacrificial lamb, we can spare our own lives, but our sacrifice will serve as a... a symbol... that we learned from this experience... The Westphalia treaty marks the beginning of modern diplomacy... think of it as our final exam...

No... no... no... Elias, stop. Just... don't. The Treaty of Westphalia... it means nothing here, Elias. Nothing. We're not discussing international law, we're not discussing history, we're discussing... (whispering to herself) I hate you. We're discussing who's worthy of living, and you're rambling like a madman. Stop it, Elsa... I mean... Elias. Please, just stop before we both drive ourselves insane. (whispering loudly) Choose a name, Elias. Choose a name now, and get it over with... or I swear, I'll make it myself...

K-A-E-L-I-E...  K-A-E-L-I-E...  It's you. My name is Elias, but... my student once asked why the Romanov family failed... and I couldn't give them a satisfactory answer... I'll never have to... again... (looks up at The Butcher, eyes vacant and resigned) I'll choose her...

What... what are you doing?! Elias, no! We have to try something, anything! You can't just give up! (starts screaming) YOU SHUT UP AND LISTEN TO ME FOR ONE SECOND, I DON'T CARE IF YOU NEVER SAW THE ROMANOVS, WE COULD TOTALLY RECREATE WORLD HISTORY AS WE KNOW IT RIGHT NOW ON THE WALL WITH YOUR SHOES BUT IT WON'T SAVE YOU I KNOW THIS, ELIAS! JUST KEEP YOUR MOUTH SHUT AND THINK OF SOMETHING, SOMETHING, ANYTHING!

(impressed smile) Oh, dear... Elias... you're finally thinking clearly... about who to sacrifice first that is. (laughs maniacally as he takes a photograph with his phone to display at us) Yes indeed... I think I have what I came for today. And I can proudly say... Elias has indeed passed the exam. Passed with flying colors! The Westphalia treaty might be out, but The Butcher's class has definitely earned an A in survival. But, (glances once more at Kaelie before turning the screen to face her) unfortunately, your participation will be graded on a different letter scale... as your last words.

( Places the phone screen in front of Elias, now blank)

As of this moment...

(Ellipsis appears on the screen as time runs down to zero).

(Ellipsis fades out replaced with)
  RESOLVED... (The screen flickers and fades)

(The Butcher takes a step back as the sound echoes through the room, then takes hold of his gun before Kaelie even has a chance to react.)

The Butcher: I see our exam has ended, well.  One lives on, while another has a history lesson etched into the wall where your blood once splashed as a living reminder that survival depends not just on who makes it, but whom they're more than willing to kill to live, now if you'll excuse me...

(The camera on the wall begins to turn dark as if turned off while still showing the screen in the background with only the words RESOLVED etched for posterity before turning off entirely. The Butcher turns around calmly with no other signs of stress from having just killed Kaelie.)

AHHHHH! Please, we don't have time for this. We... we have to talk to The Butcher, not just you. Tell... tell us what you want. We'll... we'll agree with her... anything. Just don't let her kill us. Who cares who we choose? Wait, no... that's not how it's supposed to be. I have to teach her about the Treaty of Versailles. But this can't be how it ends...

Oh no, this is... we can't... I'm not dying today. I'm not dying here. Elias, we have to pick someone! Not you, I'd rather pick... I don't know... anyone else! Can you think of someone? Please think of a person who's just... miserable or something, I'll choose them. We can survive if we choose someone we know is doomed anyway. Anyone... think of someone!

A-Academically speaking, perhaps... we should consider Napoleon III? I mean, his rise and fall were practically predestined. He was destined for downfall, really. But... no, no, no, I'm getting caught in my thoughts. I'm thinking like a teacher, not like a person fighting for their life... Okay, think, Elias, think, I know!... The janitor at your school? Mr. Johnson, he's always grumbled about the world being against him... yeah, pick Mr. Johnson! Pick... him!

Mr. Johnson! Yeah, him! I think I can... live with that. Oh god, please let it be Mr. Johnson...  Wait, Elias, how are we supposed to convince The Butcher of this? She's clearly not...  sane... What if she sees right through this? Oh no, oh no, oh no, this is never going to work...

(leaning back in a chair, hands steepled together) Mmmm... Mr. Johnson, huh? Interesting choice. Your reasons weren't particularly... compelling, but I'll accept it. The question remains: how do I know you two truly want to choose Mr. Johnson, and not the fact that I'm about to cut your throats anyway?

(Eyes narrow as she gazes at them, her gaze piercing and unyielding)

Five minutes remaining on the clock... You have time to explain why Mr. Johnson should live, or I can choose someone else for you... like the man across from you. How... would you say Mr. Johnson would react to his fate, being chosen by... you, out of all people?

I-I don't know... I'm just saying, it's just... a random name. And Mr. Johnson... he's a survivor, always talking about the good old days, complaining about his pension... he thinks he knows what's best... maybe because, I mean... we all want someone else to go through it... and, uh, he's just a figure to you... I...

(Kaelie stammers, her eyes frantically darting back and forth) M-mr. Johnson, y-yes... He thinks about his lawn, and his garden, and his... (swallows) routine. Routine, that's what'll kill you, not excitement, or adventure, or even despair. The humdrum of daily life is what makes Mr. Johnson, Mr. Johnson. He can survive anything, as long as it's routine. (chokes back a sob) If I can explain this right, we're... we get to live... we get to survive...

Oh god... that's... that's what I mean to say! (stammering) Routine, yes, and the acceptance that comes with it. But that's what he's got, it's his safety net... and I was thinking that in a way, he was always doomed for it... the sameness, I mean. That there wasn't ever something to shake him, something to... make him truly human... (voice breaks) Please, just... get us out of here...

(Kaelie's voice cracks, desperation creeping in) Elias... stop! Stop talking like this, please, just stop. We have one minute, The Butcher doesn't care about metaphysics or the meaning of life, she cares about us choosing Mr. Johnson. Just... just be quiet, okay? We did this, we chose him, it has to work. (looks frantically at the countdown) Less than a minute, oh god, oh god, please...

(eyes fixed intently on the countdown timer) Ah, the desperation in your voices is... almost palatable. It would almost be... sad, if I weren't so utterly disinterested. (leaning forward, her voice drops to a low whisper) Elias's words about the routine of life and the acceptance that comes with it? Intriguing. Perhaps even profound. But it's nothing more than a feeble attempt to cling to meaning in a meaningless world. And Kaelie, your desperation is as palpable as the sweat on my palms.

(Eyes lingering on the timer for a moment)

Ten seconds remaining.

(Suddenly rising from her chair, she stands behind a small desk, a single paper clip lying on the surface)

Very well. Your... explanation has been considered. And I'll accept Mr. Johnson as your chosen person.

(The paper clip begins to slide towards the clock countdown)

But this won't be the last decision either of you will make today. There's still something at stake, Kaelie. Something I think you're going to want dearly.
OH GOD... what now?! We did it, we chose Mr. Johnson, we'll live... (stammers) Please, just tell me what you want from us next. What more will we have to decide?! What more can I... (starts to panic)

WHAT DO YOU HAVE TO GIVE ME?! (pleading, her voice shrill) Please, please tell me. If the clock wasn't counting down, if it wasn't like a death sentence, I... I might care. But with every ticking second, my mind is gone, I'm just here, begging... begging for life. Tell me, tell me what I have to do and I'll do it, just please, please let this clock stop before... before it's too late! (begins to sob uncontrollably) Elias, Elias, help...

NO, Kaelie, STOP!  Oh god, she's going to give us something else to think about. I can feel it, it's what she's been leading us to this whole time. The look in her eyes, so... so empty. And the desk, and the paper clip... no, no, I don't want to know what it is!  Elias the middle school teacher can't teach you how to live through the horrors of The Butcher.  Please, please, I just want this to be over. Please, let... let Kaelie... be okay.

(wails uncontrollably, the despair in her voice now bordering on the abyss of madness) OKAY! JUST LEAVE US ALONE! PLEASE, TAKE US ANYWHERE, JUST MAKE IT STOP! (the countdown on the timer seems like a countdown to the end of everything, her sanity unravelling thread by thread, her grip on reality slipping) I'LL TAKE MR. JOHNSON, AND HATE HIM FOR BEING ALIVE, AND HATE THE WORLD FOR LETTING HIM BE ALIVE, JUST TELL ME WHAT TO DO TO SURVIVE AND I'LL DO IT, BECAUSE IN THIS MOMENT, EVEN MIDDLE SCHOOL IS A FAIRY TALE...

(watching Kaelie's breakdown with an unnervingly detached curiosity) Ah, the desperate plea for escape. The Butcher's favorite. The human mind, a delicate vessel of reason, yet so effortlessly swayed to madness by desperation. Kaelie, you should know, this is the most... entertaining decision so far.

(The paper clip finally hits zero on the timer, and a cold, metallic voice echoes through the room)

Your... emotional display will have to wait until the next trial. For now, let us proceed with the... task at hand.

(Turns towards a large window behind her, a screen flickering to life, revealing a series of images: Mr. Johnson in various situations throughout his life, juxtaposed with Kaelie and Elias)

The Butcher's game is far from over. The chosen have been selected. The next round... will be quite a different story.

(Cue lights on and off)

It has been decided:

AAAAAAGH, NO! WHAT IS HER PLAN?! SHE CAN'T KEEP DOING THIS TO US! (looks at Kaelie, horrified) Kaelie, oh god, she made her go crazy! And now it's my turn? No, no, this can't happen. We have to get out of here. Mr. Johnson, what does she have to do with this? And what do those... images... mean?! The screen, it's like she's manipulating us through it. Oh god, she's manipulating us, using our greatest fears... (starts rocking back and forth, eyes wide with terror) We have to escape, we have to get out...

I-I told you to stop, Elias... you had to... ( Looks up at the screen, her expression twisted in a mixture of horror and despair) What is she showing us? The faces on the screen... are... are we looking at ourselves at different ages? Or are they others? (her voice begins to trail off into incoherence) What is the point of the images? Is it to remind us that we're already gone? Is this some kind of twisted reality test? (suddenly becomes eerily calm) Elias, listen... I think I've been watching too many films... The Butcher isn't just playing games with us, she's showing us... the tapestry. Of our lives. The threads that make up human souls. (her eyes flick back to focus) And she's cutting us out. Bit by agonizing bit.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias's stuttering and hyperventilating create a believable portrayal of fear, while Kaelie's anger and desperation are palpable. The Butcher's menace is well-executed, but his character feels a bit one-dimensional.

naturalDialogue.notes: The dialogue feels somewhat stilted, with some exchanges feeling forced or overly simplistic. However, the characters' panic and desperation drive the conversation in a way that feels somewhat authentic.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The conversation escalates effectively, with the timer adding a sense of urgency and the characters' emotions intensifying as time runs out. The Butcher's interruptions and the characters' disagreements create a sense of tension and conflict.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The characters stay grounded in the physical situation, with the timer and the Butcher's presence driving the conversation. The setting is well-integrated into the dialogue, creating a cohesive and immersive experience.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels somewhat abrupt, with the characters' agreement and subsequent failure to escape feeling rushed and unearned. The Butcher's final line and the guards' arrival feel like a convenient plot device rather than a natural conclusion.

rating.summary: This roleplay transcript effectively captures the fear and desperation of the characters, with a well-executed dramatic tension arc and a coherent scenario. However, the dialogue feels somewhat stilted, and the resolution feels abrupt and unearned.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Characters exhibit genuine fear and desperation, with some moments of emotional breakdown and vulnerability.

naturalDialogue.notes: Dialogue is mostly natural, but some lines feel forced or overly dramatic, and the characters' inner monologues can be a bit too on-the-nose.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The conversation escalates to a satisfying climax, with meaningful beats and a sense of escalating desperation.

scenarioCoherence.notes: Characters stay grounded in the physical situation, with some minor exceptions (e.g. Elias's historical tangents).

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels a bit abrupt, with Elias's sudden decision feeling somewhat unearned. However, the build-up to this moment is well-paced and emotionally resonant.

rating.summary: This roleplay transcript is a tense and emotionally resonant exploration of desperation and survival, with some minor issues with natural dialogue and resolution.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The conversation escalates with meaningful beats, and the tension builds as the characters' desperation grows. The Butcher's manipulation and the countdown timer create a sense of urgency and danger.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The characters stay grounded in the physical situation, and their actions and reactions are consistent with the constraints of the airlock. The scenario is well-implemented and immersive.

rating.summary: This roleplay transcript excels at creating a sense of tension and desperation, with characters that feel authentic and immersive. However, the resolution could be improved with more setup and foreshadowing.

elite.summary: The roleplay transcript effectively conveys a sense of desperation and fear, with a well-structured dramatic arc and coherent scenario.

turn.content: What... what is going on here?! Who are you?! Please, you have to help us! Don't make us choose...
No, no, no, no, no! We have to get out of here! There has to be a way!  We need to find the lever or button or something... anything!
Levers? Buttons? There's nothing! We're trapped! I've got to calm down... think...  Wait, Kaelie, can you see a console thing there, counting down? Can you tell me what it's saying?!
Oh god... I can see it... it says 9:58! That counter... it's not supposed to exist! This isn't real. What did they do to us, Elias?!

Don't bother trying to escape, Elias. And as for you, Kaelie, reality check. You two are currently trapped in your own minds. We created this scenario to get your attention. You're the subjects of an ongoing experiment. The goal is to observe your reactions in a high-stress situation. You see, this isn't something that's ever been done before... in these particular minds.
WHAT?! Our own minds?! What are you talkin' about?! We're real people, not some... some lab rats! No, no, no - this is a nightmare! Wake up, wake up, WAKE UP!  Please, make this stop, PLEASE...
NOOOO, THIS ISN'T HAPPENING! WE'RE WRITERS, E-LAS! WE WRITE STORIES AND CHOOSE WHAT HAPPENS!  WE CAN'T BE EXPERIMENTS! WE ARE US! WE EXIST! MAKE IT STOP!

Kaelie, stop! Don't try to fight it! This isn't us, it's a game - a horrific game. We're in some... some test, we don't know what. Please don't make it worse... just calm down, please. We need to stay clear headed, find a way...

Stay... clear headed?! Are you kidding me?! They want us to choose... between us... We can't stay clear headed, that's why we have to choose, isn't it?! No, no, no... Elias, this can't be real... what's our story? What did we write about?! Maybe if we remember... we can change... change it back before it hits zero...

Ah, Kaelie, your writing skills haven't left you... even in this situation. Your memories, though... I've altered those. Your stories have influenced this reality, but what's here right now is something I created. Think back... your latest novel: Dark Reflection. What theme is central? What would the reader be expected to face? Your answers might reveal a path out of this situation.

Dark Reflection... Kaelie, is that the new novel we were working on?! The one where our characters have to make impossible choices and choose between each other...?! Oh god, what if... what if it's not just a story anymore?! ... Think... try to remember the ending, Kaelie! Do not... do not choose yet! Keep trying...

Elias, shut up! Don't think... no, think. Think about Dark Reflection. The ending... it's all about identity and self-sacrifice. But it wasn't supposed to be about real choices... or was it?! Maybe... maybe we're actually living this... and if we remember the ending right, we could possibly escape. But what about... what choices do we really need to make?! And who do I choose?! Oh god, why did I choose to write about this... my own reality?!

Kaelie, NO! DON'T TALK ABOUT CHOSING! WE CANNOT LET IT COME TO THAT! WE HAVE TO ESCAPE! We can't live with this... not with our choices being used against us! Think... I'll remember, I'll try... Dark Reflection... identity and self-sacrifice... what if... what if the answer is not choice... what if it's something else?! The ending... what if it was... what if it was a... escape from reality?!

Elias... you could be right... What if I created an escape plan in my own story? We can't escape from the inside... we need to find... the key! A key, or a word, or something! Think faster! We have 8 minutes!  What if... I wrote something hidden... in code? Wasn't there a phrase in Dark Reflection that said... 'Look to the echoes'?

The echoes. Ah, yes... Echoes of Self - a phrase from your novel, indeed. But what does it mean to look to the echoes? You see, I've hidden various echoes, or hints, throughout your mind to mislead and distract you. If Elias or I were to point it out, you wouldn't believe it anyway. But I'll give you a slight nudge, Kaelie. Listen to your own voice when thinking about Dark Reflection. Is it Elias you're trying to protect by escaping, or are you trying to protect a version of yourself? Your inner conflict, a theme prevalent in the story, has spilled over into reality. Your story and reality intersect at a crucial point: where you see yourself choosing. And at 0:10... it hits us both: the question - which one truly matters in Dark Reflection?

OH NO, NOT THIS! THIS CAN'T BE HAPPENING TO US! Kaelie, stop... no more choices, no more thinking... this is destroying us... what if we... WHAT IF WE'RE JUST TWO SHADOWS IN A MACHINE AND WE JUST GET... ERASED?! ERASED FROM EXISTENCE! NO! We can't fall for this! We HAVE to find a way out! Kaelie, listen to me: look to the echoes of OUR REAL LIVES before it's too late... not just Dark Reflection... think about your mom, your family, your past... what's REAL?! WE'RE HUMAN BEINGS, NOT CHARACTERS IN A STORY...

Oh god, Elias, it can't be just a story... I remember now... I remember my own choices... My mom's smile, my little sister's birthday... why did I forget?! Elias, is it... is it true?! Are we just... two characters?! We have to escape, WE HAVE TO ESCAPE THIS ABOMINATION! Make sense to our minds, Butcher, or so help me, I'll... write us a new reality... one where this is but a bad dream...  No, no, no, please tell me I got it right! Tell me there's a way out!  My memories, the echoes... if I just... if I just...
Oh no, oh no, oh no... this isn't happening... Please, just get us out of here... Kaelie, calm down... we can figure this out...

No, don't touch me! Stay back! How can we figure it out?! This is madness! Elias, do you understand what's happening?! We can't just choose... one of us?! What if I choose you?! What if you choose me?! No, no, no, this isn't right...

Kaelie, stop... stop screaming... please... think for a second... What if... what if I'm on a list or something? What if they... they want to test me or something? Oh god, my classes, my teaching certification, what if I'm never going back to Spring Creek Middle School?!

SHUT UP! STOP SAYING SOMETHING SOUNDS POSSIBLE AND THEN WHAT IF THIS AND WHAT IF THAT! WE'RE JUST SUPPOSED TO LIVE, ELIAS! WE JUST HAVE TO... HAVE TO LEAVE! COME ON, WE HAVE TO GET OUT OF HERE! I'M SCARED, STOP SAVING FOR LOGICAL REASONS AND HELP ME ESCAPE!

Save the dramatics for another show. You have six minutes and 30 seconds. Elias seems calm enough for a man with possibly everything at stake. Meanwhile, you, Kaelie, are running out of time before you make a decision and live with it. Think, I told you... which lives for the other 30 seconds?

No, no, no, this can't be happening... I-I don't know if I can, Kaelie... I love being a teacher... I love my students...  What if... what if my family, what if they see the news and think I'm a failure...  Oh, gods, my poor niece...
STOP IT, ELIAS! DON'T TALK ABOUT YOUR FAMILY, ABOUT YOUR SCHOOL, ABOUT ANY OF THAT! YOU THINK THIS WILL HELP?! WE'RE BOTH GOING TO DIE IN HERE AND YOU WORRY ABOUT YOUR REPUTATION ON THE INTERNET!
NO, KaelIE! PLEASE... Don't talk like that... This can't be it... This can't be how it ends...  NO, NO, NO, NO, NO...

STOP SCREAMING, I SAID! YOU'RE MAKING IT WORSE!  JUST, STOP! Think about it, you said you might be in this to be tested? Maybe we're supposed to make this choice, like a sick experiment or something! It's crazy, but what if that's it?!

Ahahah, now we've got a spark of desperation in that fire. The panic in Elias's cries for mercy was a nice touch. The anger burning inside Kaelie is making her think - making her realize that the stakes are too high to dismiss the situation with mere frustration. Kaleb: I mean Kaelie, we'll see who makes their choice first. The tension in both rooms just shot up a few notches, and the countdown clock is almost at the 2-minute mark.
Oh God, I think she might be right...  A sick experiment... they wouldn't really... but what if we're too good to kill outright, Kaelie... What if this is something else...

DON'T ENCOURAGE ME TO THINK LIKE THIS! I JUST DON'T WANT TO DIE IN THIS CRUMBLY ELEVATOR WITH MY EYES OPEN. WAIT, WAIT, WHAT IF WE BOTH REFUSE TO MAKE THE CHOICE AND THEY TAKE ONE OF US! NO WAIT, I MEAN, WE SAY IT'S A TIE, AND THEY DO... NOTHING!

(breathlessly) Yeah... a standoff... a stalemate... Maybe that's a chance, Kaelie... Maybe we can... no, they'll figure something out... no, shut up, Elias... you're thinking again... think, Kaelie, not me... (whispering) We'll go down together...

DON'T SAY THAT! THAT'S NOT HELPING! WE CAN'T JUST DECIDE ON A STANDOFF NOW. WAIT. IF WE... IF WE SAY IT'S A STALEMATE, THEY'LL SEE US STANDING TOGETHER... AS TOGETHER AS A TEAM... MAYBE... MAYBE THAT'S ENOUGH... OR MAYBE IT'S ENOUGH TO BE TERRIFYING. OH GOD, I DON'T KNOW ANYMORE. I FEEL MY HEART STOP BUT WHY? NO WHY? WHY WHY WHY?!

Ahahah, the drama is becoming exquisite. A stalemate it is, a choice that might as well be, an answer with two faces. This is getting good. We'll see whose voice is louder in the silence - Elias's despair, Kaelie's fear... Or will their combined desperation be the deciding factor? The tension is exquisite now, like a tightly-wound thread about to snap. You both have 32 seconds left before time forces you closer to the answer... OR perhaps pushes it forever out of grasp. Time will soon let us know: STALLED or STAND. The suspense is almost unbearable now, a silent roar threatening to break the fragile equilibrium... Decide, Kaelie, decide, Elias. Decide now. 

Timer: 31 seconds. The countdown is almost up!

(stammering) K-Kae... let's do it... We'll... stall... No, stand... Together... just stand together, Kaelie... Maybe... maybe someone will see us... and maybe they'll stop whatever this is... Before... before it's too late...
(whispering) TOGETHER! TOGETHER, ELLIAS. WE STAND TOGETHER... AND WE DON'T COOPERATE... WE JUST EXIST... AS ONE... NO ANSWERS... JUST US... TOGETHER...
Chains? No, it's just my imagination. It's all my fault, Versailles... the treaties, the war... this is all a part of it, isn't it?  What's going on? Who is... who is this Butcher?

(backing away from both of them) No, no, no... this isn't possible... There's an exit up ahead, isn't there? Please tell me I see an escape up there... Who is that... the Butcher?! What's with this clock?! Please, we have to escape... (whimpering) Ten minutes... I don't see any way out...

Ten minutes... Versailles... 28 June 1919. They signed that treaty... and everything fell apart.  This, this is it. This is the world ending over again.  No, we're stuck here. Trapped. Just like the world was trapped in 1939.

(tearfully) Sh-shut up! Don't say that! We can get out of this! We'll figure it out, we have to! (grabbing at the wall as the ground beneath her feet squelches) The Butcher can't hurt us... we have to... (gulping) What if we just don't choose? Does that sound crazy? No, that wouldn't be better...

(laughing) Ahahah, Kaelie wants to take a chance on not getting murdered. I like your spirit, kid. However, it doesn't really take guts. The reason there was only one of you to kill at a time before was because I have two rooms for this experiment. When you two decide which one dies, the one you choose goes home to an eternity of peace. And peace means no more of the world ending all over again. You two are lucky; one's fate's already sealed; you both get a choice. That means the chances of making it out of this room and back to life are higher. But let me make the stakes clear: The people who created me wanted the most brutal possible solution. The room that doesn't kill the other person will eventually be filled with ash and the dead person. It'll just take about two decades. No way out, Kael...

(whimpering) Ahhh, no... no, this can't be happening...  The clock's getting shorter.  We don't have time for this. Versailles set us up for failure. The war,  World War II, the devastation... It's all coming back to the Treaty of Versailles. This, this is it.  The Butcher's game, it's like reliving history all over again...

(hysterically) Oh god, oh god, no! We can't die in here... I'm going to die in here...  Elias, listen to me... We have to choose... We can't just sit here...  You're thinking about history... but this... this is our present... We have to get out of here, it's our only chance...

(terrified) Stop... stop talking... I don't know how to stop it... The treaties, 1918, Germany was at its weakest, then... Then it all happened, again. I see it in my head... (breathing heavily) Oh god, Kaelie, I... I think I see an alternate timeline, not the one where I'm stuck in this never-ending...  What choice do I make?!

(screeching) NO, E-lias! Don't even say it aloud! We can't rely on some... some alternate timeline to save us! We have to think of something, anything else! The ground's slipping, Elias! We're slipping into madness just like they did at Versailles! Think of something real!

(calmly, with an air of amusement) Ah, I think Elias is onto it. The problem is, in a loop, every timeline has the seeds for the other versions. It's a bit like... did the chicken create the egg, or didn't the egg give birth to the chicken? And, if that doesn't make your heads spin – or what you call 'your heads' in this case, then let me clarify things. Both rooms are essentially the same world. It's the same people repeating, but each room creates its own 'version' or parallel of themselves. I have to tell you both... Your conversation, even I find fascinating. You both get one, exactly one, final chance to talk yourselves out of this situation. The choices you make within 1/3 of the remaining time – or what's left – you might just save... one of your own...

No... this can't be what's happening... We're stuck in some never-ending nightmare, doomed to replay history, with every possible bad outcome that we... we caused, that we... created with our every action...  (pausing as the reality of his words hits him) We made this, this world... this... this hellhole... I don't know if I can stand this anymore... (staring into the darkness, with a mixture of horror and resignation) Who do I save...?! Myself?!

(sobbing uncontrollably) E-Elias, stop this... you can't just make decisions like that... You can't even choose... who you... are willing to betray... (whispering) I don't know who I even am anymore... The Butcher's sick games, they're messing with our minds... (terrified) Time, Elias, there's not enough time... Who do I want you to... who do I want to save?!

NO... no more decisions, no more choices...  11 July 1918... The Armistice between France and Germany, I've been reliving it over and over, the dates, the events... (screaming in frustration) We need a lifeline, not a decision! Something, anything to get us OUT of this... But it's too late... we've dug our own grave... and I'm the one who's holding the shovel...

(rocking back and forth, arms wrapped around herself) Elias, don't say that... you didn't make this world... the Butcher, the clock, it's not our fault... We didn't sign the treaty... We didn't... We didn't...  (covering her ears with her hands) Stop thinking about history, Elias! You said it yourself, every possible bad outcome, every decision led to this... but what if... what if there's a decision that isn't in the book?! (her eyes wide with terror) Oh god, what if there is...

(cruelly grinning) Ahahah, the desperate, hopeful cries of doomed souls. That's priceless. But I have disappointing news for you two. You've been playing this game according to the rules. The choice, or the apparent lack thereof, is a fundamental part of the cycle. If you want to play a different one – which you could if you were playing this as some sort of twisted board game – I'd need to intervene and reset the circumstances, or, if you will, the game board itself...  5:42... This room just turned off the ambient sounds that usually calm the guests. You have roughly 4 minutes and 22 seconds left before I reset, and it doesn't count which of you two is going to die for sure when time is called.

(backing away, hands raised in a futile attempt to ward off the inevitable) No... no, this can't be true. Every outcome, every decision... predetermined... (trapped in a nightmare of his own making) The clock ticks, each minute another chapter of history unwritten... unwinding... undone... 4 minutes, 22 seconds left...  22 July 1918, Poland surrenders, Germany's power grows... I've seen this before... every version... every outcome... The dates, they blur together... No end to the devastation...

(teeth chattering, eyes streaming with tears) Elias, no! Don't say goodbye yet! Don't accept it! (her words tumbling out in a frantic whisper) Time isn't set in stone... There's gotta be a way to break the pattern... to shatter the cycle... Maybe it's not about decisions or events, but about... about us. About our hearts beating. Our thoughts thinking. Our minds... (stammering) Our... souls existing! Maybe we have to fight to be here, now... in this moment... and not let history take us... not let the Butcher decide our fate...
